Completed: *Audit sidebar override wiring* (1/4)

**Fixed**
- Root cause was in PortalShell.tsx: the shell only rendered sidebar overrides for the Pura section, so service pages were setting custom rails that never appeared.
- I patched the shell so Services pages now actually show their page-specific sidebar content for AI Receptionist, Outbound, Newsletter, Blogs, Reviews, Nurture, and the other service clients already using the override hook.
- I updated PortalInboxClient.tsx so the left sidebar now owns the Inbox/Outbox flow: Email vs SMS selector, Email Inbox/Outbox/All mailbox selector, search, and thread list.
- Desktop inbox now uses the sidebar for thread picking; the inline thread column is hidden there, and the main pane stays focused on the conversation.

**Validation**
- Lint passes.
- Typecheck passes.
- I also cleaned the lingering hook warnings in PortalReviewsClient.tsx.

**What to do**
- Reload the portal and check the Services rail again.
- Then open Inbox / Outbox and verify the sidebar shows Email, SMS, mailbox selection, and threads.

Completed: *Audit service rail styling* (1/4)

**Updated**
- PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx now renders selector rows with icon chips, so the custom service rails look much closer to the main Services selector style instead of plain secondary buttons.
- PortalShell.tsx now treats an explicit click on the Services top icon as “show the generic Services sidebar,” even if the current service page has a custom sidebar override active.
- I polished the selectors in PortalInboxClient.tsx, PortalBlogsClient.tsx, PortalNewsletterClient.tsx, PortalReviewsClient.tsx, and PortalAiReceptionistClient.tsx so the top selectors use icon/color treatment and lists sit underneath them.

**Behavior**
- Clicking the Services icon now gives you the full service-switcher rail again.
- The top selector rows now read more like service selectors, with icons and color states instead of generic button rows.
- Lists remain below the top selectors in the service-specific rails.

**Validation**
- `npm run lint` passes.
- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passes.

- Kept newly created Pura chats from falling back to the draft view by protecting fresh thread IDs until server thread lists catch up, and by syncing local thread/message refs more defensively in PortalAiChatClient.tsx.

The two biggest regression sources are patched. I’m validating those files now and then I’ll give you exactly what changed, not a hand-wave.

Ran terminal command:  ./node_modules/.bin/eslint src/app/portal/PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx src/app/portal/PortalFloatingTools.tsx src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx

Checked [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx), [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/PortalFloatingTools.tsx), [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx), no problems found

I found concrete regressions, not just “feel” issues: the sidebar helper was adding those chunky icon chips, and both Pura chat and floating tools were still using `scrollIntoView`, which can yank the wrong scroller. I’m committing and pushing these fixes now so you can recheck the live build.

Ran terminal command:  git add src/app/portal/PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx src/app/portal/PortalFloatingTools.tsx src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx && git commit -m "Fix portal chat scroll and sidebar icon styling" && git push origin main

Completed: *Audit scroll behaviors* (1/3)

You were right — those regressions were real.

**What I fixed**
- Removed the ugly oversized icon-chip treatment from PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx; service sidebar items now use plain small inline icons again.
- Removed `scrollIntoView` from Pura chat in PortalAiChatClient.tsx and from floating tools in PortalFloatingTools.tsx. That call can pull ancestor scroll containers and is a common cause of the jumpy “wrong thing scrolled” glitch.
- Kept the scroll behavior on the actual chat scroller via direct `scrollTo`, which is much less likely to glitch the page/container hierarchy.

**Validation**
- `eslint` passed on the 3 changed files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

**Git**
- Pushed as `1639797f` with message `Fix portal chat scroll and sidebar icon styling`.

If the scroll is still glitching after this push, the next place I’ll audit is the nested overflow structure in PortalShell.tsx, because that’s the remaining likely source.

Completed: *Audit Pura scroll path* (1/4)

**Fixed**
- Reduced remaining Pura jumpiness in PortalAiChatClient.tsx by preventing route changes from scrolling the page, and by skipping redundant bottom-scroll calls when the scroller is already at the target.
- Named widget-originated chats at creation time in PortalFloatingTools.tsx, and auto-titled suggestion-only widget threads in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts) so they stop sitting around as `New chat`.
- Added explicit bug-report titles in route.ts, so report-widget records are no longer anonymous.
- Scoped portal theme behavior to the portal subtree in PortalThemeClient.tsx, globals.css, and PortalBillingClient.tsx, so portal dark mode no longer bleeds into public landing pages.

**Validation**
- `npx eslint ...` passed on the touched Pura/theme/API files.
- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.
- I did not push anything.

If you want, I can do one more pass specifically on the floating Pura widget’s scroll behavior under rapid message streaming, since that’s the other place most likely to still feel jumpy in real use.

The targeted files are clean, and the remaining diagnostics are pre-existing style nits in unchanged areas. I’m marking the audit/fix steps complete and then I’ll summarize exactly what changed.

Completed: *Map scroll entry points* (1/3)

**Scroll Audit**
- I fixed the remaining portal jump pattern at the root: internal portal navigation now uses non-scrolling transitions in PortalShell.tsx, PortalFloatingTools.tsx, SettingsTabsClient.tsx, PortalInboxClient.tsx, PortalNewsletterClient.tsx, PortalAiOutboundCallsClient.tsx, and src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/(tabs)/PortalBlogsShell.tsx/PortalBlogsShell.tsx).
- In the shell, I added a shared `PortalNavLink` so sidebar, footer, settings, services, and shortcut links stop triggering page-level scroll resets inside the nested portal layout.
- I also replaced the last portal `scrollIntoView` with nearest-scroll-container logic in SettingsTabsClient.tsx, which avoids dragging ancestor scrollers.

**Coverage**
- I normalized the remaining redirect-style flows too: page.tsx, page.tsx, PortalOnboardingClient.tsx, page.tsx, PortalBillingClient.tsx, PortalBillingUpgradeClient.tsx, PortalPeopleContactsClient.tsx, and [src/app/portal/app/services/funnel-builder/funnels/[funnelId]/edit/FunnelEditorClient.tsx](src/app/portal/app/services/funnel-builder/funnels/[funnelId]/edit/FunnelEditorClient.tsx).

**Validation**
- Targeted error checks came back clean for the files above.
- `scrollIntoView` is gone from the portal codepath.
- There are still pre-existing Tailwind-style suggestions in PortalAiOutboundCallsClient.tsx and PortalNewsletterClient.tsx; I left those alone because they’re unrelated to the scroll fix.
- I did not push anything.

Made changes.

User: You need to use the exact same size you use for the icons and the text in the sidebar selector from the services. The same size that you use for icons and the text for each service in that. You need to use it in the sidebars that are created for each service.

The styling is much closer now, but I think the sizing is still off, and you're not using the colors. You're using black for some reason for the icons. I don't know why you're doing that. Email should be that darker grayish color. It's a brand color. I don't know what to tell you. Same one we used for a book a call icon and a help icon in the header.

SMS should be blue. I'm going to screenshot something for styling for the email section. I want it to essentially look like Gmail. We should have:
- A new email button at the top
- Channels
- Inbox
- Unread
- Outbox
- Include attachments
I also want to be able to store drafts. Whatever capability you need to add to make that work will be included.
I think that’s pretty much it, but then at the top, instead of saying “Inbox”, “Outbox”, and “Email and SMS threads in one place”, we’ll just get rid of that. When we’re on email, we’ll just have the search bar at the top with the filters. Use the liquid glass styling that we use in Pura for that, the same way we use it in the header.
For the search bar, we should keep the dropdown that comes up as they’re searching, and keep their recent searches there too. We’ll also have the filter icon so they can filter by:
- who
- from
- subject
- has words in there
- has attachments
- the date
- which category they want to search in
The composer should still be a pop-up, but they should be able to control the size by dragging the left, right, top, and bottom edges and moving it around within that window. They should be able to make it smaller, but by default it should end at the left-hand side bar and not cover up the header. Right now the height is good, but it should not cover up the left-hand side bar, so we should start right after it, whether it’s expanded or collapsed. It should be dynamic, obviously.
For SMS, it should just open up to whichever was the last one they were on, so if they were on, it should open up to the last thread that they were on. I don’t like it being a pop-up on desktop; on mobile it’s fine. On desktop, no pop-up; pop-up is bad. Just have it appear in that area. Get rid of where it says “Inbox”, “Outbox”, “Email and SMS threads in one place”, and have just the same exact styling as the pop-up, but have it just on the page in that area where it would say “Choose an SMS thread”. It will never really have to say that, and the search bar will stay where it is.
All of that stuff is good. Just make sure that that’s put together. Don’t commit or push anything, and then we’ll move on, but there is still a lot of styling within these things that just isn’t that good. Just make sure that all the icons for these services, like our receptionist and outbound, whenever the sidebar changes, have the colors and the right sizing, so colors on icons and all that. Make sure that it essentially looks the same; it’s not like a huge style diff whenever we open up a service.

Completed: *Audit rail sizing/colors* (1/6)

**Updated**
- Matched the override rail icon chip sizing to the canonical shell in PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx and added service-tone support so override sidebars stop falling back to black icons.
- Applied those tones to the sidebars you called out in PortalInboxClient.tsx, PortalAiReceptionistClient.tsx, PortalAiOutboundCallsClient.tsx, PortalNewsletterClient.tsx, and PortalBlogsClient.tsx.
- Reworked Inbox in PortalInboxClient.tsx: Gmail-style sidebar nav (`New email`, `Channels`, `Inbox`, `Unread`, `Outbox`, `Include attachments`), removed the old title/description block, added a Pura-style glass search/filter header with recent searches + suggestion dropdown, and added the requested filter fields.
- Moved desktop thread browsing into the page body, made desktop SMS render inline instead of as a popup, and persisted the last-open SMS thread so it reopens where the user left off.
- Upgraded the desktop email composer to open after the portal sidebar, stay below the header, and support dragging plus left/right/top/bottom edge resizing; email drafts now persist locally for `To`, `Subject`, and `Body`.
- Validated with `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint`; no commit or push was made.

If you want, I can keep going on the remaining visual polish inside the Inbox panes themselves so the list/message styling gets even closer to Gmail.

**Updated**
- Added the new reusable globe glyph in PortalIcons.tsx and switched the live-link icon in PortalBlogsClient.tsx and PortalBookingClient.tsx.

**Media Library**
- Removed the service-specific sidebar override from PortalMediaLibraryClient.tsx so it stays on the normal shell sidebar.
- Tightened the folder/file card layout there: fewer columns, taller non-square cards, top-right action dots, and cleaner spacing so the dots no longer crash into the bottom area.

- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passes.

User: Dude, I don't know if you lost your mind or what you did, but what's up with this search bar?
- What's up with this search bar, and why does it say "No conversations yet"? What happened to the email conversations? Why does it say "No conversations yet" when there's an open conversation thread?
- What happened to my email conversations? In the email, it looks like Gmail, and the left-hand sidebar contains the conversation threads. The new email should look like the new email button in this column, but it needs to be in the left-hand sidebar, not that weird, ugly one you have there.
- Put the channels beside each other: email on the left, SMS on the right, and keep the categories (Inbox, Unread, Outbox, and Including 2 Attachments) in the same dark gray as the rest. What are we doing, dude? Why did you revert like that?
- When I click the filter thing in the search bar, it does nothing. The search bar looks stupid. Make the search bar wider so it takes up more area. It's just short like that and looks stupid, and there's just some weird gray card around it for some reason.
- Same thing for texts: why is it not in the left-hand sidebar for SMS? Why did you put it back in the page area? I didn't tell you to do that at all. I actually told you the opposite, the complete opposite.
- Whenever I'm in SMS or email, it doesn't take the left-hand sidebar back to showing services when I click Services.
- Whenever I click on Settings, it doesn't take the left-hand sidebar back to Settings when I'm in Inbox or Outbox.
- Get rid of the "Credits remaining" card in the AI receptionist; it looks horrible, like complete garbage.
- Make the test call thing taller so it looks like a phone, and get rid of the card around it.
- Get rid of the text that says "Testing" in the page area, because it's already in the left-hand sidebar.
- Missed call text back will look fine when the "Credits remaining" thing is gone.
- Same thing for Settings; it'll look fine when that credit from anything is gone.
I can't click on certain right now. When I click on Services, it doesn't open up the left-hand sidebar to Services. I don't know what you're doing. Same thing for Settings.
- AI outbound is the only one that's fine-ish.
- Get rid of that white card around the campaign, because we have one, two, three layers of cards.
- Get rid of that background card in Messages and Calls, even in Settings; it's got to go.
- It looks bad, and it just looks bad.
- Clean that up and fix all of these.
- Whenever I'm in the Services and the sidebar changes for those services, make sure I can still click the Services icon and it brings the services back on the sidebar.
- Click the Settings icon and it brings the settings back on the sidebar.
- Newsletters is fine, but get rid of where it says "Your newsletters", like that hero thing with the headline and the subheadline.
- Blogs is fine, but with the icons, what are we doing?
- Make the post icon blue.
- Make the book icon for posts blue.
- Get rid of the headline that says "Blogs" and the subheadline that says "Stay searchable without writing every week".
- The new blog button already told you this, but the new blog button needs to go under Total Credits and it needs to be blue instead of being up there at the top right, because it needs to look similar to the newsletter area.
- In Reviews, the reviews icon needs to be blue, and the requests icon can stay the color it is.
- Get rid of the card around the whole thing where it says "Reviews".
- Same thing in Requests; get rid of the card that's around it, because it looks garbage.
- Nurture campaigns is fine.
- Booking automation is fine.
- Just get rid of the "Booking automation" headline and the "Publish a booking link" subheadline thing.
- Appointment reminders is cool.
- Follow up; why does it say "Follow-up settings"? Just make it say "Follow up" instead of saying "Follow-up settings" in the left-hand sidebar.
- I don't know why you made it say "Follow-up settings".
- Just say "Follow up" and then get rid of the thing that says "Follow up automation" in "Follow-ups automatically after a booked appointment ends".
- Settings is fine-ish, but not really, because you didn't do anything I told you to do within these settings.
- What's going on here? You got two columns right now. No, not two columns. We don't want two columns. We want one thing at the top where it says "Calendars", and they pick from those calendars or they add a calendar. Once they are editing a calendar, that's when the rest of the stuff comes up as a pop-up and they click through all that stuff and do all that editing.

const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async()=>{
  const browser = await chromium.launch({headless:true, executablePath:'/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args:['--no-sandbox']});
  const page = await browser.newPage({ viewport:{width:1440,height:1200} });
  page.setDefaultTimeout(60000);
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/login?from=%2Fportal%2Fapp%2Fservices%2Finbox%2Femail', {waitUntil:'domcontentloaded'});
  if (page.url().includes('/portal/login')) {
    await page.locator('input[type="email"]').fill('demo-full@purelyautomation.dev');
    await page.locator('input[type="password"]').fill('demo12345');
    await page.getByRole('button', {name:'Sign in'}).click();
    await page.waitForURL('**/portal/app/services/inbox/email');
  }
  await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
  console.log('URL', page.url());
  const text = await page.locator('body').innerText();
  console.log(text.slice(0, 4000));
  await browser.close();
})();

(async()=>{
 const browser=await chromium.launch({headless:true, executablePath:'/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args:['--no-sandbox']});

Memory-wise, the important fixes are already in place and the broad portal runtime audit is still green. I’m now tightening the screenshot tooling itself because the saved AI capture flow is failing on stale label expectations, which is blocking fresh evidence generation more than the app is.

Ran terminal command:  export PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin && /bin/cat >/tmp/ai_tab_dump.js <<'NODE'
const fs = require('fs');
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
const BASE = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000';
function readCookie() {
  const line = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt', 'utf8').split('\n').find((e) => e.includes('\tpa.portal.session\t'));
  if (!line) throw new Error('cookie missing');
  return line.split('\t')[6];
}
(async () => {
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true, executablePath: '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args: ['--no-sandbox'] });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
  await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: readCookie(), domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, secure: false, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
  const page = await context.newPage();
  page.setDefaultTimeout(45000);
  const urls = [
    ['activity', `${BASE}/portal/app/services/ai-receptionist`],
    ['testing', `${BASE}/portal/app/services/ai-receptionist?tab=testing`],
    ['settings', `${BASE}/portal/app/services/ai-receptionist?tab=settings`],
  ];
  for (const [name, url] of urls) {
    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' });
    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    const text = await page.locator('main').innerText().catch(() => '');
    console.log(`===== ${name} =====`);
    console.log(text.slice(0, 2000).replace(/\n+/g, '\n'));
  }
  await browser.close();
})().catch((err) => {
  console.error(err);
  process.exit(1);
});

- Cleared the stale .next output and repeatedly shut down stray dev/capture processes, including the final local server, so they stop chewing memory.

- Updated the capture tooling in capture-review-shots.mjs so it no longer false-times-out on hidden duplicate text, hanging font readiness, or the huge Booking follow-up full-page shot.
- The previously attached AI Receptionist folder now has fresh captures in ai-receptionist.

**Validation**
- The broad portal runtime audit still passes in report.json.
- The review-shot manifest has current successful entries across the service set in manifest.json.

- Best next move: fully reload VS Code once so the new watcher exclusions take effect and the bloated renderer/tsserver state gets reset.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import fs from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';

const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const cookieFile = '/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt';
const outFile = path.join(process.cwd(), 'tmp', 'portal-qa', 'review-shots-loaded', 'booking', '06-settings.png');
const cookieLine = fs.readFileSync(cookieFile, 'utf8').split('\n').find((entry) => entry.includes('\tpa.portal.session\t'));
if (!cookieLine) throw new Error('Missing pa.portal.session cookie');
const cookieValue = cookieLine.split('\t')[6];

const browser = await chromium.launch({
  headless: true,
  executablePath: '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
  args: ['--no-sandbox'],
});
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookieValue, domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, secure: false, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
const page = await context.newPage();
page.setDefaultTimeout(120000);
await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/booking?tab=settings`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
await page.getByText('Calendars', { exact: true }).waitFor({ state: 'visible' });
await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Edit selected calendar', exact: true }).waitFor({ state: 'visible' });
await page.evaluate(async () => {
  if (document.fonts?.ready) {
    await Promise.race([document.fonts.ready, new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 3000))]);
  }
});
await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(outFile), { recursive: true });
await page.locator('main').screenshot({ path: outFile });
console.log(`Saved ${outFile}`);
await context.close();
await browser.close();
NODE

Why would we have the display of the calendar and have the calendar there where we select again? It's just redundant; it makes no sense.

Make sure that it's selected, and then whenever we go into bookings, we'll just carry on whichever calendar is selected. Also give them an opportunity to select a calendar in bookings as well. Whichever one they select in bookings will carry on to if they click back on appointments and vice versa. Grab me a screenshot of bookings, and I'll look in there too. But let's see. I can't really see how the selected one looks, but it just needs to be highlighted blue, with no outline around it. We need an outline around it. If there are bookings and stuff, then it will have a pop-up when that day is selected that will show all the bookings and stuff on that day.

Availability looks fine; that one's cool. Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node <<'NODE'
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async () => {
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true, executablePath: '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args: ['--no-sandbox'] });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
  const fs = require('fs');
  const line = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt', 'utf8').split('\n').find((entry) => entry.includes('\tpa.portal.session\t'));
  const cookieValue = line.split('\t')[6];
  await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookieValue, domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);

  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/booking', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Today', exact: true }).click({ force: true, timeout: 30000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(500);
  await page.evaluate(() => {
    const rail = document.querySelector('[data-testid="booking-week-scroll"]');
    const selected = rail?.querySelector('[data-selected="true"]');
    if (!(rail instanceof HTMLElement) || !(selected instanceof HTMLElement)) return;
    const targetLeft = selected.offsetLeft - rail.clientWidth / 2 + selected.clientWidth / 2;
    rail.scrollLeft = Math.max(0, targetLeft);
  });
  await page.waitForTimeout(500);
  const weekState = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const rail = document.querySelector('[data-testid="booking-week-scroll"]');
    const selected = rail?.querySelector('[data-selected="true"]');
    if (!(rail instanceof HTMLElement) || !(selected instanceof HTMLElement)) {
      return { selectedFound: false };
    }
    const railRect = rail.getBoundingClientRect();
    const selectedRect = selected.getBoundingClientRect();
    return {
      selectedFound: true,
      scrollLeft: rail.scrollLeft,
      selectedText: selected.textContent?.slice(0, 80) ?? '',
      fullyVisible: selectedRect.left >= railRect.left && selectedRect.right <= railRect.right,
      overlapsViewport: selectedRect.left < window.innerWidth && selectedRect.right > 0,
    };
  });

  const bookingsPage = await context.newPage();
  await bookingsPage.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/booking?tab=bookings', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
  await bookingsPage.locator('text=/Loading…|Loading\.\.\./').first().waitFor({ state: 'hidden', timeout: 60000 }).catch(() => {});
  await bookingsPage.waitForTimeout(1000);
  const bookingsState = await bookingsPage.evaluate(() => ({
    body: document.body.innerText,
    loadingVisible: /Loading…|Loading\.\.\./.test(document.body.innerText),
  }));

  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    weekState,
    bookingsState: {
      loadingVisible: bookingsState.loadingVisible,
      hasUpcoming: bookingsState.body.includes('Upcoming bookings.'),
      hasEmptyState: bookingsState.body.includes('No upcoming bookings.'),
      hasReschedule: bookingsState.body.includes('Reschedule'),
    },
  }, null, 2));

  await browser.close();
})();

const fs = require('node:fs');
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async () => {
  const line = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt','utf8').split('\n').find((entry) => entry.includes('\tpa.portal.session\t'));
  if (!line) throw new Error('Missing pa.portal.session cookie');
  const cookieValue = line.split('\t')[6];
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true, executablePath: '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args: ['--no-sandbox'] });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
  await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookieValue, domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/booking', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    url: page.url(),
    title: await page.title(),
    body: (await page.locator('body').innerText()).slice(0, 2000),
    todayButtons: await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Today', exact: true }).count(),
    h1s: await page.locator('h1').allInnerTexts().catch(() => [])
  }, null, 2));
  await browser.close();
})();

Ran terminal command:  node -e 'const fs=require("node:fs"); const {chromium}=require("@playwright/test"); (async()=>{const line=fs.readFileSync("/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt","utf8").split("\n").find(v=>v.includes("\tpa.portal.session\t")); if(!line) throw new Error("Missing pa.portal.session cookie"); const cookieValue=line.split("\t")[6]; const browser=await chromium.launch({headless:true, executablePath:"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome", args:["--no-sandbox"]}); const context=await browser.newContext({viewport:{width:1440,height:1200}}); await context.addCookies([{name:"pa.portal.session", value:cookieValue, domain:"127.0.0.1", path:"/", httpOnly:true, sameSite:"Lax"}]); const page=await context.newPage(); await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/booking",{waitUntil:"domcontentloaded",timeout:60000}); await page.waitForTimeout(3000); const body=(await page.locator("body").innerText()).slice(0,1200); const todayButtons=await page.getByRole("button",{name:"Today",exact:true}).count(); const h1s=await page.locator("h1").allInnerTexts().catch(()=>[]); console.log(JSON.stringify({url:page.url(), title:await page.title(), todayButtons, h1s, body},null,2)); await browser.close(); })().catch(err=>{console.error(String(err?.stack||err)); process.exit(1);});'

import fs from 'node:fs';
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
const line = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/purely_portal_cookies.txt', 'utf8').split('\n').find((entry) => entry.includes('\tpa.portal.session\t'));
const cookieValue = line.split('\t')[6];
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true, executablePath: '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome', args: ['--no-sandbox'] });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookieValue, domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/inbox/email', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Mail filters', exact: true }).click({ force: true, timeout: 30000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
const result = await page.evaluate(() => ({
  body: (document.body.textContent || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').slice(0, 1200),
  buttons: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).map((button) => ({
    text: (button.textContent || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(),
    visible: (() => {
      const style = window.getComputedStyle(button);
      const rect = button.getBoundingClientRect();
      return style.display !== 'none' && style.visibility !== 'hidden' && Number(style.opacity || '1') > 0 && rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0;
    })(),
  })).filter((item) => item.visible).slice(0, 80),
}));
console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));
await browser.close();
EOF

Completed: *Fix root cause* (2/3)

**Fixed**
- I found and patched a real shared-domain bug in vercelProjectDomains.ts: Vercel can return `400` for “this domain is not on the project yet,” and our code treated that like a hard failure instead of proceeding to add the domain.
- I also added support for `VERCEL_TEAM_SLUG` in addition to `VERCEL_TEAM_ID`, which makes team-scoped Vercel projects more reliable for user domain provisioning.
- Because funnels, blogs, newsletters, reviews, and other hosted surfaces all reuse the same custom-domain provisioning path, this fix improves the whole custom-domain connection flow, not just Funnel Builder.

**Validated**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
- `npm run lint -- src/lib/vercelProjectDomains.ts` passes.
- Updated env-var docs in README.md.

**What changed**
- Shared helper hardening: vercelProjectDomains.ts
- Env docs: README.md

**Important**
- This is fixed in code locally, but to confirm it for real users, it still needs to be deployed and then tested by adding/verifying a real custom domain in the live app.
- If you want, I can do the next step too: commit this fix, push it, and then walk through a production verification checklist with you.

The build is clean, so I’m doing the safe provider probe next: a read-only Vercel API check against a guaranteed-missing domain to confirm our new missing-domain handling matches the real API.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import fs from 'node:fs';

function loadEnvFile(path) {
  const text = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8');
  for (const rawLine of text.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const line = rawLine.trim();
    if (!line || line.startsWith('#')) continue;
    const eq = line.indexOf('=');
    if (eq <= 0) continue;
    const key = line.slice(0, eq).trim();
    let value = line.slice(eq + 1).trim();
    if ((value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) || (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))) {
      value = value.slice(1, -1);
    }
    if (!(key in process.env)) process.env[key] = value;
  }
}

loadEnvFile('.env.local');

const token = (process.env.VERCEL_API_TOKEN || process.env.VERCEL_TOKEN || '').trim();
const project = (process.env.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID || process.env.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID_OR_NAME || process.env.VERCEL_PROJECT_NAME || '').trim();
const teamId = (process.env.VERCEL_TEAM_ID || '').trim();
const teamSlug = (process.env.VERCEL_TEAM_SLUG || '').trim();

if (!token || !project) {
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: false, reason: 'missing-env', hasToken: !!token, hasProject: !!project }));
  process.exit(0);
}

const probeDomain = `pa-probe-${Date.now()}.example.com`;
const base = `https://api.vercel.com/v9/projects/${encodeURIComponent(project)}/domains/${encodeURIComponent(probeDomain)}`;
const scopes = [
  teamId ? `?teamId=${encodeURIComponent(teamId)}` : null,
  teamSlug ? `?slug=${encodeURIComponent(teamSlug)}` : null,
  '',
].filter((v, i, arr) => v !== null && arr.indexOf(v) === i);

const results = [];
for (const qp of scopes) {
  const res = await fetch(`${base}${qp}`, {
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
  }).catch((error) => ({ ok: false, status: null, json: async () => ({ fetchError: String(error?.message || error) }) }));
  const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
  results.push({
    scope: qp || '(none)',
    status: 'status' in res ? res.status : null,
    ok: 'ok' in res ? res.ok : false,
    code: body?.error?.code ?? body?.code ?? null,
    message: body?.error?.message ?? body?.message ?? body?.error ?? null,
    verified: typeof body?.verified === 'boolean' ? body.verified : null,
  });
}

console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, probeDomain, results }, null, 2));
NODE

**What I verified**

- `npm run lint -- src/lib/vercelProjectDomains.ts` passes.
- `npm run build` passes end-to-end.
- A read-only Vercel API probe against your configured project succeeded and showed that, in this environment, a missing project domain returns `404` with `Project Domain not found.`

**Why this is stronger now**
- The helper still handles the real `404` behavior your project returns today.
- It now also safely handles alternative “missing domain” shapes via `400`, which makes it less brittle across Vercel account/project variations.
- It now supports both team scoping styles: `VERCEL_TEAM_ID` and `VERCEL_TEAM_SLUG`.

**Extra confidence check**
- I ran a mocked smoke test locally against the helper logic to prove:
- a “missing domain via 400” path still proceeds to add/verify correctly
- team-slug-scoped requests work correctly

**Bottom line**
- Locally, this is in a very good state: compile-clean, lint-clean, build-clean, provider behavior checked, and edge-case paths exercised.
- The next meaningful validation would be a real deployed add/verify flow against an actual custom domain, but short of that, this is about as hardened as I can make it locally.

**Fixed**
- Patched capture_pura_audit.mjs so it no longer hangs on unbounded API calls or Playwright `networkidle` waits.
- Added hard per-request, per-navigation, and per-scenario timeouts; each scenario now fails independently instead of stalling the whole run.
- Fixed a Playwright setup bug that leaked the browser process before cleanup, which is why the old run could appear stuck forever.

**Verified**
- Syntax check passes.
- Re-ran the script under a hard 8-minute Python timeout.
- It completed successfully and exited cleanly: screenshots were saved to pura-audit-shots.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && python3 - <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request
from http.cookiejar import CookieJar
from urllib.request import build_opener, HTTPCookieProcessor, Request

BASE = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'
EMAIL = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'
PASSWORD = 'pura-demo-local'

jar = CookieJar()
opener = build_opener(HTTPCookieProcessor(jar))

def open_json(url, data=None, headers=None):
    req = Request(url, data=data, headers=headers or {})
    with opener.open(req, timeout=45) as res:
        return json.loads(res.read().decode())

login_body = json.dumps({'email': EMAIL, 'password': PASSWORD}).encode()
login = open_json(f'{BASE}/portal/api/login', login_body, {
    'content-type': 'application/json',
    'x-portal-variant': 'portal',
})
print('login_ok', login.get('ok'))
for path in [
    '/api/portal/funnel-builder/funnels',
    '/api/portal/funnel-builder/forms',
    '/api/portal/newsletter/newsletters?kind=external&take=10',
    '/api/portal/booking/settings',
]:
    try:
        data = open_json(f'{BASE}{path}')
    except Exception as e:
        print('\n===', path, '===')
        print('ERROR', repr(e))
        continue
    print('\n===', path, '===')
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:5000])
PY

Ran terminal command:  python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess, sys, time
cmd = ['node', 'tmp/portal-qa/capture_pura_audit.mjs']
proc = subprocess.Popen(cmd, cwd='/Users/jaylan/purely-automation')
timeout = 720
start = time.time()
while True:
    rc = proc.poll()
    if rc is not None:
        print(f'EXIT {rc} after {time.time()-start:.1f}s')
        sys.exit(rc)
    if time.time() - start > timeout:
        proc.kill()
        print(f'TIMEOUT after {timeout}s')
        sys.exit(124)
    time.sleep(2)
PY

import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
BASE='http://127.0.0.1:3000'
email='demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'
password='pura-demo-local'
req=urllib.request.Request(BASE+'/portal/api/login', data=json.dumps({'email':email,'password':password}).encode(), headers={'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, method='POST')
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as resp:
    body=json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    cookie=resp.headers.get('set-cookie','')
if not body.get('ok'):
    raise SystemExit('login failed')
import re
m=re.search(r'pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)', cookie)
if not m:
    raise SystemExit('no cookie')
session=m.group(1)
req=urllib.request.Request(BASE+'/api/portal/ai-chat/threads', headers={'cookie':f'pa.portal.session={session}'}, method='GET')
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as resp:
    body=json.loads(resp.read().decode())
threads=body.get('threads') or []
for t in threads[:12]:
    print(json.dumps({
        'id': t.get('id'),
        'title': t.get('title'),
        'updatedAt': t.get('updatedAt'),
        'lastMessageAt': t.get('lastMessageAt'),
        'lastCanvasUrl': (t.get('contextJson') or {}).get('lastCanvasUrl') if isinstance(t.get('contextJson'), dict) else None,
        'unresolvedRun': (t.get('contextJson') or {}).get('unresolvedRun') if isinstance(t.get('contextJson'), dict) else None,
    }, ensure_ascii=False))

Ran terminal command:  cat > /Users/jaylan/purely-automation/tmp/portal-qa/list_recent_threads.py <<'PY'
import json
import re
import urllib.request

BASE = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'
EMAIL = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'
PASSWORD = 'pura-demo-local'

login_req = urllib.request.Request(
    BASE + '/portal/api/login',
    data=json.dumps({'email': EMAIL, 'password': PASSWORD}).encode(),
    headers={'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal'},
    method='POST',
)
with urllib.request.urlopen(login_req, timeout=60) as resp:
    login_json = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    cookie_header = resp.headers.get('set-cookie', '')

if not login_json.get('ok'):
    raise SystemExit('login failed')

match = re.search(r'pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)', cookie_header)
if not match:
    raise SystemExit('no cookie')

session = match.group(1)
threads_req = urllib.request.Request(
    BASE + '/api/portal/ai-chat/threads',
    headers={'cookie': f'pa.portal.session={session}'},
    method='GET',
)
with urllib.request.urlopen(threads_req, timeout=60) as resp:
    threads_json = json.loads(resp.read().decode())

threads = threads_json.get('threads') or []
for thread in threads[:16]:
    context = thread.get('contextJson') if isinstance(thread.get('contextJson'), dict) else {}
    print(json.dumps({
        'id': thread.get('id'),
        'title': thread.get('title'),
        'updatedAt': thread.get('updatedAt'),
        'lastCanvasUrl': context.get('lastCanvasUrl'),
        'unresolvedRun': context.get('unresolvedRun'),
    }, ensure_ascii=False))
PY

**Progress**
- Tightened and hardened the audit runner in capture_pura_audit.mjs: auto-confirms high-impact actions, carries canvas context, supports scenario filtering, and records screenshot failures as warnings instead of losing the scenario.
- Fixed the newsletter intent/resolution path across puraDirectIntentSignals.ts, puraResolver.ts, portalAgentActions.ts, and portalAgentActionExecutor.ts.

**What changed**
- Booking now completes cleanly to `/portal/app/services/booking/settings` in the audit.
- Funnel edit stays on the real funnel editor, and funnel creation completes after one auto-confirm.
- Newsletter no longer creates a brand-new draft by mistake; it now resolves the existing-draft flow, updates the target draft content/title/excerpt, and saves audit evidence in manifest.json.
- Remaining rough edge: the newsletter flow still logs a noisy initial “not found/get failed” step before succeeding on the update, and the newsletter service screenshot is intermittently slow.

const BASE='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const email='demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password='pura-demo-local';
function parseCookie(v){const m=String(v||'').match(/pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)/);return m?m[1]:null;}
const login = await fetch(`${BASE}/portal/api/login`, {method:'POST', headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, body:JSON.stringify({email,password})});
const loginJson = await login.json();
const cookie = parseCookie(login.headers.get('set-cookie'));
if(!login.ok || !loginJson?.ok || !cookie) throw new Error('login failed');
const res = await fetch(`${BASE}/api/portal/newsletter/newsletters?kind=external&take=10`, {headers:{cookie:`pa.portal.session=${cookie}`}});
const json = await res.json();
console.log(JSON.stringify((json.newsletters||[]).slice(0,8).map(n=>({id:n.id, slug:n.slug, title:n.title, status:n.status, createdAt:n.createdAtIso, updatedAt:n.updatedAtIso})), null, 2));
NODE

**Fixed**
- Hardened the audit runner in capture_pura_audit.mjs: bounded confirm handling, bounded auto-continue nudges, rejection of manual step-by-step replies, scenario filtering, scenario-specific timeouts, exact service start URLs, and screenshot failures recorded as warnings instead of collapsing results.
- Fixed newsletter intent/resolution regressions in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts, puraResolver.ts, portalAgentActions.ts, and portalAgentActionExecutor.ts.
- Fixed booking settings arg handling so Pura-generated aliases like `slug` and `duration` no longer die at schema/normalization time in portalAgentActions.ts and portalAgentActionExecutor.ts.

**Current audit state**
- `booking` is now clean: latest full audit updates the duration to `45` minutes and lands on `/portal/app/services/booking/settings`.
- `create funnel` is much better: latest targeted run creates the funnel and landing page on the real funnel canvas, but full-run consistency is still shaky.
- `newsletter` no longer hangs and does update the draft content, but Pura still emits a noisy initial “couldn’t retrieve the draft” step before succeeding.
- `edit existing funnel` remains the weakest flow: Pura still sometimes drifts into Inbox despite explicit Funnel Builder context.

**Artifacts**
- Latest audit evidence is in pura-audit-shots and the most recent manifest is manifest.json.

**Bottom line**
- I fixed the concrete local code issues I found and materially improved the audit harness.
- The remaining problems are now mostly real Pura behavior issues in funnel editing/creation and a noisy newsletter retrieval step, not hanging/tooling bugs.

If you want, I can keep pushing specifically on the funnel-planner behavior next.

I’ve narrowed it to runtime behavior, not detection. Next I’m reproducing the exact API call outside the browser harness so we can see the raw chat response for a create-funnel prompt.

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node <<'NODE'
const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const email='demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password='pura-demo-local';
function parseCookie(setCookie){const m=String(setCookie||'').match(/pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)/); return m?.[1]||'';}
const run=async()=>{
  const loginRes=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`,{method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'},body:JSON.stringify({email,password})});
  const loginJson=await loginRes.json();
  const cookie=parseCookie(loginRes.headers.get('set-cookie'));
  if(!loginRes.ok||!loginJson?.ok||!cookie) throw new Error(`login failed ${loginRes.status} ${JSON.stringify(loginJson)}`);
  const threadRes=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/ai-chat/threads`,{method:'POST',headers:{cookie:`pa.portal.session=${cookie}`,'content-type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify({title:'tmp reproduce funnel direct',chatMode:'work',responseProfile:'fast'})});
  const threadJson=await threadRes.json();
  if(!threadRes.ok||!threadJson?.ok) throw new Error(`thread failed ${threadRes.status} ${JSON.stringify(threadJson)}`);
  const threadId=threadJson.thread.id;
  const prompt='Create a new funnel called Webinar Audit Sprint 202604120400 for consultants who want more booked strategy calls. Do the creation directly in Funnel Builder and keep the funnel in draft.';
  const msgRes=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/${encodeURIComponent(threadId)}/messages`,{method:'POST',headers:{cookie:`pa.portal.session=${cookie}`,'content-type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify({chatMode:'work',responseProfile:'fast',url:`${BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat/${threadId}`,text:prompt})});
  const msgJson=await msgRes.json();
  console.log(JSON.stringify({status:msgRes.status, threadId, response:msgJson}, null, 2));
};
run().catch(err=>{console.error(err); process.exit(1);});
NODE

**Done**
- Fixed the funnel-edit drift at the route layer in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts):
  - context-only “I’m on this exact Funnel Builder page” prompts now anchor the current funnel/page instead of triggering generation
  - current-page edit prompts now go straight to `funnel_builder.pages.generate_html` from the active `canvasUrl`
- Added a dedicated current-page funnel-edit signal in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts.
- Added the matching direct plan in puraDirectIntentPlans.ts.

**Validated**
- Reproduced the full 3-turn edit flow successfully in repro_edit_funnel_flow_after_patch.json:
  - turn 1 anchors Funnel Builder context
  - turn 2 updates the page via `funnel_builder.pages.generate_html`
  - turn 3 keeps working on the same funnel page instead of drifting to Inbox
- Create-funnel remains clean from earlier validation and targeted audit output in tmp/portal-qa/pura-audit-shots/manifest.json.
- Checked for diagnostics on the edited source files; no new errors were reported.

**Note**
- The screenshot audit manifest was still showing an older stale edit-funnel run while the fresh rerun was in progress, but the raw post-patch repro confirms the root funnel-routing issue is fixed.
- I also created a few temporary repro helpers under tmp to isolate the behavior quickly.

Updated todo list

**Current State**
- Funnel edit is green again in isolated audit runs; the latest thread stays on Funnel Builder for anchor → hero update → form embed.
- Funnel create is green in isolation too; the last retry completed successfully after an earlier transient login/runtime failure.
- Newsletter edit is fixed and now completes cleanly without the old draft-resolution failure path.
- Booking is fixed in both turns: the first reply now returns both the settings editor and live `/book/...` link, and the follow-up duration check no-ops correctly when it is already `45`.

**What I Changed**
- Added executor-side newsletter fallback resolution in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts so `newsletter.newsletters.get/update/send` can resolve by title/slug/current-draft hint instead of depending entirely on planner ID resolution.
- Added booking live-link handling in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts so `booking.site.get` exposes an explicit public booking URL.
- Tightened booking planning in puraPlanner.ts for requests that ask for both editor view and live booking link.
- Added deterministic booking preflight handling in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts) for:
  - booking settings surface updates that need editor + live link
  - conditional “if it’s not already 45 minutes, change just that” follow-ups

**Validation**
- Latest targeted booking thread now shows:
  - first turn: settings updated + editor link + live link
  - second turn: “already set to 45 minutes” no-op
- Latest targeted newsletter audit passes cleanly.
- Latest targeted funnel edit and funnel create audits pass cleanly.

**Remaining Note**
- A direct full-suite pass still showed intermittent funnel scenario timeouts once, but server output also showed a Prisma `P2024` connection-pool timeout during that period. The latest isolated funnel reruns passed, so the remaining instability looks runtime-related rather than a current funnel logic bug.

If you want, I can continue by hardening the full-suite reliability next—either by tracing the Prisma `P2024` source or by making the audit harness more resilient to transient runtime pressure.

Completed: *Define shared editor model* (2/4)

**Done**
- I turned the audit into a concrete build plan in hosted-page-editor-unification-plan.md.
- Recommended architecture: reuse the funnel editor engine and block system, but do not reuse the `CreditFunnel` tables for hosted pages.
- Instead, add a shared `HostedPageDocument` model for layout/presentation, while keeping booking/newsletter/reviews/blogs APIs as the source of truth for business data.

**Why this path**
- Funnel pages already have the right persistence shape: `editorMode`, `blocksJson`, `customHtml`.
- Hosted pages currently store mostly settings-shaped data, so forcing them into funnel entities would mix service ownership with funnel semantics and get messy fast.
- A shared hosted-page document layer gives Pura one universal editing surface while preserving all existing service actions.

**Build order**
- Start with reviews first, then booking, then newsletter/blogs.
- Add a shared hosted-page action family for Pura, while keeping existing actions like `booking.form.update` and `blogs.appearance.update`.

If you want, I can take the next step and scaffold the actual Prisma model plus the first hosted-page CRUD endpoints for reviews.

**Done**
- The hosted-page unification plan is now fully implemented and the plan status is updated in hosted-page-editor-unification-plan.md.
- Shared hosted-page persistence, lifecycle helpers, and preview/runtime data are in hostedPageDocuments.ts; shared Pura coverage is in portalAgentActions.ts and portalAgentActionExecutor.ts.
- Shared editing is live through HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx, with booking/newsletter/reviews/blog editor routes wired under the portal service pages.
- Public hosted rendering is live for booking, reviews, newsletters, blog index, and blog post templates across both slug routes and custom-domain routes; the bad block-renderer call sites were fixed.
- The old reviews-specific editor file at HostedReviewsPageEditorClient.tsx is no longer duplicate logic; it now just wraps the shared editor.

- `npm run build` now completes successfully, including TypeScript and Next.js production build.

Made changes.

User: Does this change the UI at all for users? Meaning, how does it look for them to edit their hosted pages? 

GitHub Copilot: Yes — for portal users, this adds a new editing UI for hosted pages.

**What Changes**
- Users now get an `Edit page` entry from each hosted service area:
  - booking in PortalBookingClient.tsx
  - newsletter in PortalNewsletterClient.tsx
  - reviews in PortalReviewsClient.tsx
  - blogs in PortalBlogsClient.tsx
- Clicking that opens a dedicated shared editor screen built by HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx.

**How It Looks For Them**
- It’s a full-page editor, not a tiny settings modal.
- Top area: back link, page title, save button, and export button.
- Left side: document/page selector, title/slug/status/editor-mode controls, content area for either blocks JSON or custom HTML, plus a “Generate with Pura” prompt box.
- Right side: live preview pane of the hosted page.
- Supported modes are `BLOCKS`, `CUSTOM_HTML`, and stored `MARKDOWN`, with the current UX mainly focused on blocks/custom HTML.

**What Does Not Change**
- The normal booking/reviews/newsletter/blog settings screens still exist.
- Existing customers’ public pages do not visually change unless they edit/publish a hosted page document.
- Service logic still works the same; this mainly adds a new layout-editing surface on top.

**Net Effect**
- For users editing hosted pages: yes, definite UI change — they now have a proper shared page editor.
- For end visitors on public pages: only changes if the business customizes/publishes the hosted page.

If you want, I can also give you a quick “user journey” walkthrough of exactly what someone clicks and sees from each service screen.

const BASE='http://localhost:3000';
function extractCookie(setCookieHeader){ if(!setCookieHeader) return ''; const first=String(setCookieHeader).split(',').find(p=>p.includes('='))||''; return first.split(';')[0]?.trim()||''; }
const loginRes = await fetch(`${BASE}/portal/api/login`, { method:'POST', headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, body: JSON.stringify({email:'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password:'pura-demo-local'}) });
const loginJson = await loginRes.json().catch(()=>null);
const cookie = extractCookie(loginRes.headers.get('set-cookie'));
console.log('login', loginRes.status, !!loginJson?.ok, cookie ? 'cookie-ok' : 'no-cookie');
for (const service of ['reviews','booking','newsletter','blogs']) {
  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents?service=${service}`, { headers:{ cookie } });
  const text = await res.text();
  console.log('\nSERVICE', service, 'STATUS', res.status);
  console.log(text.slice(0,800));
}
NODE

**Verdict**
- I ran two live Pura audit suites against the local app and saved the transcripts/results in pura_hosted_pages_audit_results.json and pura_hosted_pages_literal_audit_results.json, using run_pura_hosted_pages_audit.mjs and run_pura_hosted_pages_literal_audit.mjs.
- Overall, it is not good yet for this feature: across 16 hosted-page prompts, Pura hit zero hosted_pages.documents actions, executed zero real hosted-page mutations, and mostly fell back to generic “go to this screen and click this” guidance.

**What Pura Did**
- Natural-language prompts about hosted pages were mostly misrouted into old service logic: reviews used only reviews.site.get, newsletter used only newsletter.site.get, and blogs even returned an unrelated prerequisite error about publishing a draft.
- Literal/tool-style prompts like “List my hosted page documents for REVIEWS” still produced no hosted-page action trace at all; the runTrace was empty on every prompt in the second pass.
- Mutation prompts such as publish, reset, and generate HTML were not carried out; Pura answered with made-up manual instructions instead of doing the work.

**Quality Assessment**
- Strengths: responses were polite, safe, and did not accidentally perform destructive actions.
- Weaknesses: action selection is currently poor, answers hallucinate UI steps, and she does not yet reason in the shared hosted-page model you just added.
- Practical grade: response polish is maybe B-, but actual hosted-page task competence is F right now.

**Backend Note**
- There is also a live dev-path issue: the hosted-page list route at route.ts is returning 500 in the running dev server, and the crash is inside the bootstrap upsert path at hostedPageDocuments.ts.
- Prisma migration status says the database is up to date, so this looks more like a runtime/dev-server issue than missing migrations.

**Bottom Line**
- The hosted-page feature itself is implemented, but Pura is not actually using it yet in a reliable way.
- If you want, I can go straight into fixing this next: first the hosted-pages 500 in dev, then the Pura routing/resolver prompts so she actually chooses the hosted_pages.documents tool family.

}

- Updated PortalAiChatClient.tsx so fresh “New chat” starter chips now rotate across openings, penalize recently shown prompts, and persist recent chip history locally; this stops the same media/marketing prompts from dominating every empty chat.

- `npm run build` passes.

Checked [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/connect/%5BroomId%5D/ConnectRoomClient.tsx), [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/api/connect/rooms/%5BroomId%5D/signal/route.ts), no problems found

**Update**
- I kept iterating and landed two real product fixes:
- Restored-room resumes now proactively restart polling and participant refresh in [src/app/connect/[roomId]/ConnectRoomClient.tsx](src/app/connect/%5BroomId%5D/ConnectRoomClient.tsx).
- Broadcast media signals now accept `toParticipantId: null` in [src/app/api/connect/rooms/[roomId]/signal/route.ts](src/app/api/connect/rooms/%5BroomId%5D/signal/route.ts), which removes the earlier `400` signal failures.

**Where it’s blocked**
- The screenshot harness in connect_meeting_preview.mjs now gets farther: I reproduced real join responses and a true in-call “Connecting…” state.
- The remaining blocker is unstable meeting formation under automation: runs alternate between second-user `/join` `500`s, stuck “Joining…”, and “Trying to connect to Morgan” without the peer fully establishing.
- I also confirmed this is not just a bad screenshot script; there’s underlying instability in the Connect join/signaling path when driven by multi-browser automation.

**Net**
- Code fixes are in place and clean.
- Requested final screenshots are still not ready because I can’t honestly call the current captures “actual meeting” screenshots yet.

If you want, I can keep pushing on the backend join failure next, or switch to a manual browser-driven capture pass to get the screenshots faster.

Made changes.

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node <<'EOF'
const { chromium } = await import('@playwright/test');
const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3001';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1400 } });
const res = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'DemoPortal123!' }, failOnStatusCode: false, timeout: 90000 });
if (!res.ok()) throw new Error(`login ${res.status()}`);
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/newsletter/external`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(20000);
const data = await page.evaluate(() => ({
  url: location.href,
  buttons: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).map((b) => (b.textContent || '').trim()).filter(Boolean).slice(0,120),
  body: (document.body?.innerText || '').split('\n').map((x) => x.trim()).filter(Boolean).slice(0,120),
}));
console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
await browser.close();
EOF

- Fixed the broken newsletter editor route in layout.tsx so `page-editor` no longer gets redirected as a fake audience slug.
- Rebuilt the shared hosted editor in HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx into a cleaner editor-style shell: sidebar auto-collapses, floating tools hide, the ugly generator/system-prompt UI is gone, code panes are no longer black, and preview/editor now feel much closer to the funnel builder.

const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async()=>{
  const browser = await chromium.launch({headless:true});
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport:{width:1560,height:1280}});
  const res = await context.request.post('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/api/login', { headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, data:{email:'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password:'DemoPortal123!'}, failOnStatusCode:false, timeout:90000 });
  console.log('login', res.status());
  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/newsletter/page-editor', {waitUntil:'domcontentloaded', timeout:60000});
  await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
  console.log('title', await page.title());
  console.log('save buttons', await page.getByRole('button', { name: /save changes/i }).count());
  console.log('loading count', await page.getByText(/Loading newsletter page editor/i).count());
  console.log('body text sample', (await page.locator('body').innerText()).slice(0,1200));
  await browser.close();
})();
EOF

(async()=>{
  const login = await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/api/login', {method:'POST', headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, body:JSON.stringify({email:'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password:'DemoPortal123!'})});
