Ran terminal command:  cat > /Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/components/HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx <<'EOF'
"use client";

import Link from "next/link";
import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";
import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";

import { useSetPortalSidebarOverride } from "@/app/portal/PortalSidebarOverride";
import { AiSparkIcon } from "@/components/AiSparkIcon";
import { PortalListboxDropdown } from "@/components/PortalListboxDropdown";
import { useToast } from "@/components/ToastProvider";
import { coerceBlocksJson, renderCreditFunnelBlocks, type CreditFunnelBlock } from "@/lib/creditFunnelBlocks";

type HostedEditorService = "BOOKING" | "NEWSLETTER" | "REVIEWS" | "BLOGS";

type HostedPageDocument = {
  id: string;
  service: HostedEditorService;
  pageKey: string;
  title: string;
  slug: string | null;
  status: "DRAFT" | "PUBLISHED";
  contentMarkdown: string;
  editorMode: "MARKDOWN" | "BLOCKS" | "CUSTOM_HTML";
  blocksJson: CreditFunnelBlock[];
  customHtml: string;
  customChatJson: unknown;
  seoTitle: string | null;
  seoDescription: string | null;
  themeJson: unknown;
  dataBindingsJson: unknown;
  updatedAt: string;
};

type HostedListResponse = {
  ok: boolean;
  documents?: HostedPageDocument[];
  error?: string;
};

function prettyJson(value: unknown) {
  try {
    return JSON.stringify(value ?? [], null, 2);
  } catch {
    return "[]";
  }
}

function serviceQueryValue(service: HostedEditorService) {
  return service.toLowerCase();
}

function classNames(...xs: Array<string | false | null | undefined>) {
  return xs.filter(Boolean).join(" ");
}

export function HostedServicePageEditorClient({
  service,
  serviceLabel,
  backHref,
  defaultPageKey,
}: {
  service: HostedEditorService;
  serviceLabel: string;
  backHref: string;
  defaultPageKey?: string;
}) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const toast = useToast();
  const setSidebarOverride = useSetPortalSidebarOverride();
  const appBase = String(pathname || "").startsWith("/credit") ? "/credit/app" : "/portal/app";

  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);
  const [documents, setDocuments] = useState<HostedPageDocument[]>([]);
  const [selectedDocumentId, setSelectedDocumentId] = useState("");
  const [title, setTitle] = useState("");
  const [slug, setSlug] = useState("");
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<HostedPageDocument["status"]>("DRAFT");
  const [editorMode, setEditorMode] = useState<HostedPageDocument["editorMode"]>("BLOCKS");
  const [markdown, setMarkdown] = useState("");
  const [customHtml, setCustomHtml] = useState("");
  const [blocksText, setBlocksText] = useState("[]");
  const [puraPrompt, setPuraPrompt] = useState("");
  const [puraBusy, setPuraBusy] = useState(false);

  const selectedDocument = useMemo(
    () => documents.find((entry) => entry.id === selectedDocumentId) ?? null,
    [documents, selectedDocumentId],
  );

  const syncFromDocument = useCallback((doc: HostedPageDocument | null) => {
    if (!doc) return;
    setTitle(doc.title || "");
    setSlug(doc.slug || "");
    setStatus(doc.status);
    setEditorMode(doc.editorMode);
    setMarkdown(doc.contentMarkdown || "");
    setCustomHtml(doc.customHtml || "");
    setBlocksText(prettyJson(doc.blocksJson));
  }, []);

  const replaceDocument = useCallback(
    (nextDoc: HostedPageDocument, htmlOverride?: string) => {
      setDocuments((current) => current.map((entry) => (entry.id === nextDoc.id ? nextDoc : entry)));
      setSelectedDocumentId(nextDoc.id);
      syncFromDocument({
        ...nextDoc,
        customHtml: htmlOverride ?? nextDoc.customHtml,
      });
    },
    [syncFromDocument],
  );

  const loadDocuments = useCallback(async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents?service=${serviceQueryValue(service)}`, { cache: "no-store" });
      const data = (await res.json().catch(() => null)) as HostedListResponse | null;
      if (!res.ok || !data?.ok || !Array.isArray(data.documents)) {
        throw new Error(data?.error || `Failed to load ${serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} hosted page documents`);
      }

      const nextDocuments = data.documents;
      const preferred =
        (defaultPageKey ? nextDocuments.find((entry) => entry.pageKey === defaultPageKey) : null) ?? nextDocuments[0] ?? null;

      setDocuments(nextDocuments);
      if (!preferred) throw new Error(`No ${serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} hosted page document found`);
      setSelectedDocumentId(preferred.id);
      syncFromDocument(preferred);
    } catch (error) {
      toast.error(`Could not load ${serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} page editor\n${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Please try again."}`);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }, [defaultPageKey, service, serviceLabel, syncFromDocument, toast]);

  useEffect(() => {
    void loadDocuments();
  }, [loadDocuments]);

  useEffect(() => {
    setSidebarOverride({ forceCollapsed: true });

    const root = document.documentElement;
    root.setAttribute("data-pa-hide-floating-tools", "1");

    return () => {
      setSidebarOverride(null);
      root.removeAttribute("data-pa-hide-floating-tools");
    };
  }, [setSidebarOverride]);

  const parsedBlocks = useMemo(() => {
    try {
      return coerceBlocksJson(JSON.parse(blocksText));
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  }, [blocksText]);

  const saveDocument = useCallback(async () => {
    if (!selectedDocument) return;

    let nextBlocks: CreditFunnelBlock[] | undefined;
    if (editorMode === "BLOCKS") {
      if (!parsedBlocks) {
        toast.error("Blocks JSON is invalid\nFix the JSON before saving.");
        return;
      }
      nextBlocks = parsedBlocks;
    }

    setBusy(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents/${encodeURIComponent(selectedDocument.id)}`, {
        method: "PATCH",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          title,
          slug: slug.trim() || null,
          status,
          editorMode,
          contentMarkdown: markdown,
          customHtml,
          ...(nextBlocks ? { blocksJson: nextBlocks } : null),
        }),
      });
      const data = (await res.json().catch(() => null)) as { ok?: boolean; document?: HostedPageDocument; error?: string } | null;
      if (!res.ok || !data?.ok || !data.document) {
        throw new Error(data?.error || `Failed to save ${serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} page`);
      }
      replaceDocument(data.document);
      toast.success(`${serviceLabel} page saved`);
    } catch (error) {
      toast.error(`Save failed\n${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Please try again."}`);
    } finally {
      setBusy(false);
    }
  }, [customHtml, editorMode, markdown, parsedBlocks, replaceDocument, selectedDocument, serviceLabel, slug, status, title, toast]);

  const exportBlocksToHtml = useCallback(
    async (opts?: { silent?: boolean }) => {
      if (!selectedDocument) return null;
      if (!parsedBlocks) {
        toast.error("Blocks JSON is invalid\nFix the JSON before exporting HTML.");
        return null;
      }

      setBusy(true);
      try {
        const res = await fetch(`/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents/${encodeURIComponent(selectedDocument.id)}/export-custom-html`, {
          method: "POST",
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          body: JSON.stringify({ title, blocksJson: parsedBlocks, setEditorMode: "CUSTOM_HTML" }),
        });
        const data = (await res.json().catch(() => null)) as { ok?: boolean; html?: string; document?: HostedPageDocument; error?: string } | null;
        if (!res.ok || !data?.ok || !data.document) {
          throw new Error(data?.error || "Failed to export custom HTML");
        }
        const html = data.html || data.document.customHtml || "";
        replaceDocument(data.document, html);
        if (!opts?.silent) toast.success("Custom HTML refreshed");
        return { document: data.document, html };
      } catch (error) {
        toast.error(`Export failed\n${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Please try again."}`);
        return null;
      } finally {
        setBusy(false);
      }
    },
    [parsedBlocks, replaceDocument, selectedDocument, title, toast],
  );

  const runPura = useCallback(async () => {
    if (!selectedDocument) return;
    const cleanPrompt = puraPrompt.trim();
    if (!cleanPrompt) {
      toast.error("Add a prompt first\nTell Pura what to change on this page.");
      return;
    }

    setPuraBusy(true);
    try {
      let currentHtml = customHtml || selectedDocument.customHtml || "";

      if (editorMode === "BLOCKS") {
        const exported = await exportBlocksToHtml({ silent: true });
        if (!exported) throw new Error("Failed to prepare the page for Pura");
        currentHtml = exported.html;
      }

      const res = await fetch(`/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents/${encodeURIComponent(selectedDocument.id)}/generate-html`, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ prompt: cleanPrompt, currentHtml }),
      });
      const data = (await res.json().catch(() => null)) as { ok?: boolean; document?: HostedPageDocument; html?: string; question?: string; error?: string } | null;
      if (!res.ok || !data?.ok || !data.document) {
        throw new Error(data?.error || `Failed to update the ${serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} page with Pura`);
      }

      replaceDocument(data.document, data.html || data.document.customHtml || currentHtml);
      setPuraPrompt("");
      if (data.question) {
        toast.info(`Pura needs one detail\n${data.question}`);
      } else {
        toast.success("Pura updated the page");
      }
    } catch (error) {
      toast.error(`Pura update failed\n${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Please try again."}`);
    } finally {
      setPuraBusy(false);
    }
  }, [customHtml, editorMode, exportBlocksToHtml, puraPrompt, replaceDocument, selectedDocument, serviceLabel, toast]);

  if (loading) {
    return <div className="px-6 py-10 text-sm text-zinc-600">Loading {serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} page editor…</div>;
  }

  if (!selectedDocument) {
    return <div className="px-6 py-10 text-sm text-red-600">Could not load the {serviceLabel.toLowerCase()} hosted page document.</div>;
  }

  const documentOptions = documents.map((doc) => ({
    value: doc.id,
    label: doc.pageKey === doc.title ? doc.pageKey : `${doc.title} · ${doc.pageKey}`,
  }));

  const modeOptions: Array<{ value: HostedPageDocument["editorMode"]; label: string }> = [
    { value: "BLOCKS", label: "Blocks" },
    { value: "CUSTOM_HTML", label: "Custom HTML" },
    { value: "MARKDOWN", label: "Markdown" },
  ];

  const inputClassName = "h-11 w-full rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm text-zinc-900 outline-none transition focus:border-(--color-brand-blue)";
  const codeEditorClassName =
    "min-h-[34rem] w-full rounded-2xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-4 py-3 text-sm text-zinc-900 outline-none transition focus:border-(--color-brand-blue)";
  const monoEditorClassName = `${codeEditorClassName} font-mono text-[13px] leading-6`;
  const previewEmptyClassName = "flex min-h-[34rem] items-center justify-center px-8 text-center text-sm text-zinc-500";
  const surfaceClassName = "rounded-2xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white";
  const toolbarButtonClassName =
    "inline-flex h-11 items-center justify-center rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-4 text-sm font-semibold text-zinc-900 transition hover:bg-zinc-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50";
  const blueButtonClassName =
    "inline-flex h-11 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-(--color-brand-blue) px-4 text-sm font-semibold text-white transition hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50";

  return (
    <div data-hosted-page-editor-root={service.toLowerCase()} className="min-h-screen bg-[#f5f7fb] p-3 sm:p-4">
      <div className="mx-auto max-w-450 space-y-3">
        <div className={`${surfaceClassName} p-3`}>
          <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
            <Link href={`${appBase}${backHref}`} className={toolbarButtonClassName}>
              ← Back
            </Link>

            <div className="min-w-60 flex-1">
              <PortalListboxDropdown
                value={selectedDocumentId}
                onChange={(value) => {
                  const nextId = String(value || "");
                  setSelectedDocumentId(nextId);
                  syncFromDocument(documents.find((entry) => entry.id === nextId) ?? null);
                }}
                options={documentOptions}
                placeholder="Choose a hosted page"
                buttonClassName="flex h-11 w-full items-center justify-between gap-2 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm text-zinc-900 hover:bg-zinc-50"
              />
            </div>

            <input
              value={title}
              onChange={(event) => setTitle(event.target.value)}
              className="h-11 min-w-56 flex-1 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm text-zinc-900 outline-none transition focus:border-(--color-brand-blue)"
              placeholder={`${serviceLabel} page`}
            />

            <input value={slug} onChange={(event) => setSlug(event.target.value)} className={`${inputClassName} sm:w-56`} placeholder="Slug override" />

            <PortalListboxDropdown
              value={status}
              onChange={(value) => setStatus(String(value || "DRAFT") as HostedPageDocument["status"])}
              options={[
                { value: "DRAFT", label: "Draft" },
                { value: "PUBLISHED", label: "Published" },
              ]}
              placeholder="Select status"
              buttonClassName="flex h-11 min-w-[10rem] items-center justify-between gap-2 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold text-zinc-900 hover:bg-zinc-50"
            />

            <button type="button" onClick={() => void exportBlocksToHtml()} disabled={busy || puraBusy || editorMode !== "BLOCKS"} className={toolbarButtonClassName}>
              Export blocks to HTML
            </button>

            <button type="button" onClick={() => void saveDocument()} disabled={busy || puraBusy} className={blueButtonClassName}>
              {busy ? "Saving…" : "Save changes"}
            </button>
          </div>

          <div className="mt-3 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
            {modeOptions.map((option) => {
              const active = editorMode === option.value;
              return (
                <button
                  key={option.value}
                  type="button"
                  onClick={() => setEditorMode(option.value)}
                  className={classNames(
                    "inline-flex h-10 items-center justify-center rounded-xl px-3 text-sm font-semibold transition",
                    active ? "border border-(--color-brand-blue) bg-blue-50 text-blue-800" : "border border-zinc-200 bg-white text-zinc-900 hover:bg-zinc-50",
                  )}
                >
                  {option.label}
                </button>
              );
            })}

            <div className="ml-auto flex min-w-[20rem] flex-1 items-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white px-3 py-2">
              <AiSparkIcon className="h-4 w-4 shrink-0 text-(--color-brand-blue)" />
              <input
                value={puraPrompt}
                onChange={(event) => setPuraPrompt(event.target.value)}
                className="min-w-0 flex-1 bg-transparent text-sm text-zinc-900 outline-none placeholder:text-zinc-400"
                placeholder="Ask Pura to update this page"
              />
              <button type="button" onClick={() => void runPura()} disabled={busy || puraBusy} className={blueButtonClassName}>
                {puraBusy ? "Working…" : "Pura"}
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="grid gap-3 xl:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1.05fr)_minmax(420px,0.95fr)]">
          <section className={`${surfaceClassName} overflow-hidden p-3`}>
            {editorMode === "CUSTOM_HTML" ? (
              <textarea value={customHtml} onChange={(event) => setCustomHtml(event.target.value)} className={monoEditorClassName} spellCheck={false} />
            ) : editorMode === "MARKDOWN" ? (
              <textarea value={markdown} onChange={(event) => setMarkdown(event.target.value)} className={codeEditorClassName} spellCheck={false} />
            ) : (
              <>
                <textarea value={blocksText} onChange={(event) => setBlocksText(event.target.value)} className={monoEditorClassName} spellCheck={false} />
                {!parsedBlocks ? <div className="mt-3 text-sm font-semibold text-rose-600">Blocks JSON is invalid.</div> : null}
              </>
            )}
          </section>

          <aside className={`${surfaceClassName} overflow-hidden p-3`}>
            <div className="overflow-hidden rounded-2xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white">
              {editorMode === "CUSTOM_HTML" ? (
                <iframe
                  title={`${serviceLabel} page preview`}
                  className="h-[78vh] min-h-128 w-full bg-white"
                  sandbox="allow-same-origin"
                  srcDoc={customHtml || "<div style='padding:24px;font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;color:#64748b'>No custom HTML yet.</div>"}
                />
              ) : editorMode === "BLOCKS" ? (
                parsedBlocks ? (
                  <div className="h-[78vh] min-h-128 overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden bg-white p-6">
                    {renderCreditFunnelBlocks({ blocks: parsedBlocks, basePath: "" })}
                  </div>
                ) : (
                  <div className={previewEmptyClassName}>Fix the blocks JSON to render the page preview.</div>
                )
              ) : markdown.trim() ? (
                <div className="h-[78vh] min-h-128 overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden bg-white px-6 py-5 whitespace-pre-wrap text-sm leading-7 text-zinc-800">
                  {markdown}
                </div>
              ) : (
                <div className={previewEmptyClassName}>Add markdown content to preview this page draft.</div>
              )}
            </div>
          </aside>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
EOF

The terminal write got chewed up, so I’m taking the safer route: create a clean temp file with the right content, then atomically move it over the broken component.

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && UI_REVIEW_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 node --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
const baseUrl = process.env.UI_REVIEW_BASE_URL;
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1560, height: 1280 } });
const res = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'DemoPortal123!' }, failOnStatusCode: false, timeout: 90000 });
if (!res.ok()) throw new Error(`login failed ${res.status()}`);
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/newsletter/page-editor`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(3000);
const info = await page.evaluate(() => {
  const topbar = document.querySelector('.pa-portal-topbar');
  if (!topbar) return { exists: false };
  const rect = topbar.getBoundingClientRect();
  const style = window.getComputedStyle(topbar);
  return {
    exists: true,
    text: (topbar.textContent || '').trim().slice(0, 200),
    rect: { x: rect.x, y: rect.y, width: rect.width, height: rect.height },
    display: style.display,
    visibility: style.visibility,
    opacity: style.opacity,
    ariaHidden: topbar.getAttribute('aria-hidden'),
    hiddenAttr: document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-pa-portal-topbar-hidden'),
    cssVar: getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--pa-portal-topbar-height').trim(),
  };
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(info, null, 2));
await page.close();
await context.close();
await browser.close();
NODE

I need to see what the authenticated browser is actually landing on for the newsletter editor, so I’m probing it with a tiny Playwright script instead of guessing from the timeout.

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext();
const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3001';
const res = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, {
  headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' },
  data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'DemoPortal123!' },
  failOnStatusCode: false,
  timeout: 90000,
});
console.log('login', res.status(), await res.text());
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/newsletter/page-editor`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 90000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
console.log('url', page.url());

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node - <<'NODE'
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async () => {
  const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3001';
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
  context.on('response', async (res) => {
    const url = res.url();
    if (url.includes('/newsletter/page-editor') || url.includes('/api/portal/hosted-pages') || url.includes('/purely/newsletters')) {
      console.log('RESPONSE', res.status(), url);
    }
  });
  const loginRes = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, {
    headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' },
    data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'DemoPortal123!' },
    failOnStatusCode: false,
    timeout: 90000,
  });
  console.log('LOGIN', loginRes.status());
  const page = await context.newPage();
  page.on('console', (msg) => console.log('CONSOLE', msg.type(), msg.text()));
  page.on('pageerror', (err) => console.log('PAGEERROR', err.message));
  try {
    await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/newsletter/page-editor`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 180000 });
    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
    console.log('TITLE', await page.title());
    console.log('URL', page.url());
    console.log('HAS_ROOT', await page.locator('[data-hosted-page-editor-root="newsletter"]').count());
    console.log('BODY_SNIPPET', (await page.locator('body').innerText()).slice(0, 1200));
  } catch (error) {
    console.log('ERROR', error && error.message ? error.message : String(error));
  } finally {
    await browser.close();
  }
})();
NODE

**Done**
- Fixed the booking preview root cause in hostedPageDocuments.ts: it was calling a nonexistent Prisma model for calendar counts, which made booking preview data return `null` and forced the placeholder card.
- Expanded legacy refresh detection in hostedPageDocuments.ts so the stale newsletter/blog `1 / 2 / 3` seeded docs auto-refresh to runtime blocks instead of keeping old filler content.
- Updated custom-HTML token preview rendering in HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx so runtime tokens like `{{REVIEWS_APP}}` now show live embedded previews rather than fake dashed placeholders.
- Removed the redundant newsletter route gate in page.tsx, which was the extra route-level blocker behind the flaky/500 newsletter capture.

**Validated**
- Re-ran live inspection: booking preview now returns valid data, and newsletter/blog documents now contain `hostedNewsletterArchive` / `hostedBlogsArchive` blocks instead of the old filler columns.
- Captured fresh proof images for all four editors: manifest.json, including booking-runtime-full.png, newsletter-runtime-full.png, blogs-runtime-full.png, and reviews-runtime-full.png.

**Booking fix**
- Booking now creates one hosted page document per calendar via hostedPageDocuments.ts and hostedPageKeys.ts.
- The booking editor route now opens the specific calendar page when `calendarId` or `pageKey` is present in page.tsx.
- The “Edit page” button in PortalBookingClient.tsx now targets the selected calendar instead of the generic booking page.
- Public calendar routes now prefer the matching calendar-specific hosted document in [src/app/book/[slug]/c/[calendarId]/page.tsx](src/app/book/[slug]/c/[calendarId]/page.tsx) and [src/app/book/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/page.tsx](src/app/book/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/page.tsx).

**Capture note**
- The new booking screenshot was captured from the calendar-specific editor route shown in the manifest: `/portal/app/services/booking/page-editor?pageKey=booking_calendar_Test`.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node <<'EOF'
const { chromium } = await import('@playwright/test');
const fs = await import('node:fs/promises');
const path = await import('node:path');
const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3001';
const email = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password = 'DemoPortal123!';
const outputDir = path.resolve('tmp/review-shots/runtime-preview-pages');
const slugify = (value) => String(value || '').trim().toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '') || 'template';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 }, deviceScaleFactor: 1 });
const loginRes = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, data: { email, password }, failOnStatusCode: false, timeout: 90000 });
if (!loginRes.ok()) throw new Error(`login failed ${loginRes.status()} ${await loginRes.text()}`);
await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true });
async function fetchDocuments(service) {
  const res = await context.request.get(`${baseUrl}/api/portal/hosted-pages/documents?service=${encodeURIComponent(service)}`, { headers: { 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, failOnStatusCode: false, timeout: 90000 });
  if (!res.ok()) throw new Error(`docs failed ${service} ${res.status()} ${await res.text()}`);
  const json = await res.json().catch(() => null);
  return (Array.isArray(json?.documents) ? json.documents : []).sort((a,b)=> String(a?.title||'').localeCompare(String(b?.title||'')) || String(a?.pageKey||'').localeCompare(String(b?.pageKey||'')));
}
async function waitForEditor(page, service, route) {
  await page.goto(`${baseUrl}${route}`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 180000 });
  await page.locator(`[data-hosted-page-editor-root="${service}"]`).first().waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 180000 });
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /^templates$/i }).first().waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 180000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(1200);
}
async function capture(page, file) { const p = path.join(outputDir, file); await page.screenshot({ path: p, animations: 'disabled', fullPage: true }); return p; }
async function openTemplates(page, service) { await page.getByRole('button', { name: /^templates$/i }).first().click(); const modal = page.locator(`[data-hosted-templates-modal="${service}"]`).first(); await modal.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 180000 }); return modal; }
async function closeTemplates(page) { const overlay = page.locator('[data-overlay-root="true"]').last(); await overlay.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 180000 }); await overlay.locator('button[aria-label="Close"]').first().click(); await page.waitForTimeout(400); }
const page = await context.newPage();
const services = [
  ['newsletter','/portal/app/services/newsletter/page-editor'],
  ['blogs','/portal/app/services/blogs/page-editor'],
  ['reviews','/portal/app/services/reviews/page-editor'],
];
for (const [service, route] of services) {
  const docs = await fetchDocuments(service);
  await waitForEditor(page, service, route);
  await openTemplates(page, service);
  await capture(page, `${service}-templates-modal.png`);
  await closeTemplates(page);
  for (const doc of docs) {
    const modal = await openTemplates(page, service);
    await modal.locator('button').filter({ hasText: String(doc.title || doc.pageKey || '') }).first().click();
    await page.waitForTimeout(600);
    await closeTemplates(page);
    await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'instant' }));
    await page.waitForTimeout(900);
    await capture(page, `${service}-${slugify(doc.pageKey || doc.title)}.png`);
  }
}
await browser.close();
console.log(outputDir);
EOF

Yes — right now that image is a real image block, but it is not automatically pulling from each user’s media library yet.

- In the hosted templates, I seeded those hero images with fixed default paths like `/brand/Purely_Connect.png` and `/brand/1.png` so the templates would look complete out of the box. You can see that in hostedPageDocuments.ts and hostedPageDocuments.ts.
- Once the template is applied, that block is just an `image` block with a stored src value. The editor currently exposes that as an “Image URL” field, so it can be changed, but today it’s manual in this hosted editor. See HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx.
- On render, the page simply outputs an `<img>` using whatever src is saved on that block. There’s no special “template-only image” behavior after that. See creditFunnelBlocks.ts.

So the short version is:

- Today: default seeded image
- After editing: whatever image URL the block is set to
- Not yet: automatic “pick from this user’s media library” in this hosted page editor

The good news is the architecture supports that direction cleanly, because the app already has media-library upload/storage plumbing in portalMediaUploads.ts. So the right product behavior is exactly what you said: the template can start with a placeholder/default, but the user should be able to choose or upload their own media-library image and have that replace it.

If you want, I can make the hosted page editor’s image block work like:
- `Choose from Media Library`
- `Upload image`
- auto-set that selected media item as the block image

That would make these template visuals feel right instead of hardcoded.

Completed: *Trace editor Pura flow* (1/5)

**Done**
- Added shared hosted-template intent mapping in hostedPageTemplateIntents.ts so requests like “minimal blog”, “journalistic blog”, “newsletter editorial”, “reviews concierge”, and “minimal booking” resolve to the correct page keys.
- Updated the hosted editor flow in HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx so the in-editor Pura prompt now targets the matching template document before generating changes, instead of only editing whatever document happened to be selected.
- Updated direct Pura routing in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts so the separate Pura window now recognizes natural requests like “make a minimal blog page” or “make my newsletter editorial” and sends the correct hosted page action with the right pageKey.
- Hardened the hosted-page generator prompt in hostedPageDocuments.ts so it explicitly prefers publish-ready customer-facing copy and avoids meta/template/designer language.

**Validation**
- Targeted hosted template routing smoke passed for blog, newsletter, reviews, and booking style prompts.
- Existing Pura intent smoke passed: npm run pura:intent-smoke.
- Fresh TypeScript check passed: npx tsc --noEmit.
- Lint still fails for unrelated pre-existing em-dash issues outside this change, including [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts) and portalAgentActionExecutor.ts.

If you want, I can do the next pass on the remaining awkward seeded copy so the defaults themselves sound more publish-ready too.

User: No, I just really want you to let me know how the tests went. How do the tests go? How does Pura respond? What does Pura build? Are you satisfied completely with the way that Pura is working with these new editors, or what? 

GitHub Copilot: **Short Answer**
- The tests went well on the routing and editor-integration side.
- I am satisfied that Pura now knows which hosted template to target.
- I am not yet 100% satisfied with the live content-writing quality until I run a few real end-to-end prompts through the actual model in both surfaces.

**What I Actually Tested**
- I added and tested shared template-intent mapping in hostedPageTemplateIntents.ts.
- I verified direct Pura intent routing in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts and puraDirectIntentPlans.ts.
- I verified the hosted editor’s in-editor Pura flow in HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx.
- I verified the hosted generator prompt rules in hostedPageDocuments.ts.
- TypeScript passed clean with a fresh run.
- The existing intent smoke test passed: 8/8.
- My targeted hosted-template smoke test passed for:
  - “make a minimal blog page” → blogs_minimal
  - “make the blog look journalistic” → blogs_journal
  - “make this newsletter editorial” → newsletter_editorial
  - “make this reviews page concierge” → reviews_concierge
  - “make a minimal booking page” → booking_minimal_clinic

**How Pura Responds Now**
- In the hosted editor:
  - If you ask for a style like minimal, journal, editorial, concierge, launchpad, featured, bold, aftercare, or community, it now picks the matching hosted page document first.
  - Then it generates full updated custom HTML for that document.
  - If the page was in blocks mode, it first exports blocks to HTML, then edits the HTML.
- In the separate Pura chat:
  - The direct-intent planner now resolves those same style requests to the correct hosted page action plus the correct pageKey.
  - So instead of vaguely editing “the blog page,” it can now target the right template variant.

**What Pura Builds**
- For hosted pages, it builds a full updated HTML document for the selected hosted page.
- It is now explicitly told to use publish-ready, customer-facing copy and avoid template/meta/designer language.
- If your request is ambiguous, the hosted generator is instructed to ask one short follow-up instead of guessing.

**Am I Fully Satisfied?**
- On template targeting and endpoint flow: yes, mostly.
- On actual model output quality in live use: not completely yet.
- Reason: I validated the planner, routing, page-key resolution, compile health, and prompt rules, but I did not run a deep real-model browser roundtrip for multiple prompts and inspect the exact generated copy in each editor surface.

**My Honest Read**
- The plumbing now looks right.
- The main risk left is not “can Pura hit the right template?” anymore.
- The main risk left is “does the live model always write copy that feels production-ready enough for your taste?”

If you want, I can do the next practical step: run real prompt trials in the hosted editors and the separate Pura window, inspect exactly what it writes, and tighten anything that still sounds off.

Completed: *Rerun real Pura suite* (3/4)

**Done**
- Patched hosted-template routing in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts, hostedPageTemplateIntents.ts, and [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts) so hosted-page intents win over stray blog/newsletter/funnel signals.
- Added typo tolerance for prompts like `mak`, `minimul`, `jurnalistic`, and stopped `publish-ready` from falsely triggering blog-publish prerequisites.
- Fixed the editor QA harness in run_hosted_editor_pura_live_tests.mjs to wait for the actual post-send UI state.

**Live Results**
- Separate Pura chat now routes the real failing prompts to `hosted_pages.documents.generate_html` with the correct docs: `blogs_minimal`, `blogs_journal`, `newsletter_editorial`, `booking_minimal_clinic`, and `reviews_concierge`. Results: pura_hosted_template_live_results.json.
- In-editor Pura now does the same, including the previously wrong newsletter editorial case. Results: hosted_editor_pura_live_results.json.
- One minor quality note remains: the first editor blog-minimal case still asked a clarifying question while updating the correct doc; routing is fixed, but that copy behavior could be tightened further.
- The unrelated vague `ai_chat.threads.update` case in the separate-chat suite still fails and was not part of the hosted-template fix.

If you want, I can tighten that remaining blog clarification behavior next and clean up the stale `tsc` task state.

**Shipped**
- Hardened portalAssistantLinks.ts so assistant URLs always normalize to `https://purelyautomation.com/...`, including relative `/portal/...` paths and even `localhost` / `127.0.0.1` links.
- Added a text-level sanitizer that rewrites leaked bare portal paths and markdown links before assistant text is stored or returned.
- Wired that sanitizer into portalAgentActionExecutor.ts, portalAiChatScheduled.ts, and [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts).
- Hardened the model prompts in the action-summary and chat-summary paths so they explicitly forbid bare `/portal/app/...` output and require full `https://purelyautomation.com/...` URLs.

**Validated**
- Direct helper check now converts:
  - `/portal/app/ai-chat?thread=abc` → `https://purelyautomation.com/portal/app/ai-chat?thread=abc`
  - `http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/profile` → `https://purelyautomation.com/portal/app/profile`
- Re-ran the live Pura suite in pura_hosted_template_live_results.json; the previously bad relative-link leak path no longer emits bare portal slugs.
- File diagnostics are clean for the patched files via editor error checks.

**Note**
- The `tsc` task output in the terminal still looks stale/noisy, but direct file diagnostics for the touched files are clean. If you want, I can clean up that task-state weirdness next and keep hardening the remaining “production ready” quality edges.

**Added**
- Added a deterministic regression suite in pura-production-smoke.cjs covering hosted-template routing, typo handling, and absolute `https://purelyautomation.com/...` link normalization.
- Upgraded run_pura_hosted_template_live_tests.mjs and run_hosted_editor_pura_live_tests.mjs from “print results” scripts into actual failing smoke tests with assertions for correct page keys and no leaked relative/localhost links.
- Added runnable scripts in package.json: `pura:production-smoke`, `pura:hosted-live-smoke`, `pura:editor-live-smoke`, and `pura:all-smoke`.

**Status**
- `npm run pura:production-smoke` passes.
- `npm run pura:hosted-live-smoke` passes.
- `npm run pura:editor-live-smoke` passes after I stabilized the harness with fresh-page isolation and transient login retries.

**Risk Found**
- The live tests exposed intermittent Prisma pool starvation in dev: background portal requests can trigger temporary login `500`s. I made the smoke harnesses resilient to that, but the underlying app-side pool/load issue is still the next real production-hardening target.

If you want, I’ll tackle that Prisma/background-request contention next.

Completed: *Stabilize final validation* (3/3)

**Done**
- Fixed the booking preview-data fail-open path in hostedPageDocuments.ts so missing Prisma model shape no longer causes hosted editor `500`s.
- Fixed the repeated React missing-key warning in PortalShell.tsx by giving sidebar service items a stable key.
- Stabilized the flaky live editor smoke in run_hosted_editor_pura_live_tests.mjs so it waits on the actual editor controls it uses, not the brittle `Save changes` button visibility.

**Validated**
- `npm run pura:all-smoke` passes end to end.
- New server-log lines no longer show the `PortalShell` key warning, and booking `preview-data` now returns `200`.
- Diagnostics are clean for PortalShell.tsx, hostedPageDocuments.ts, and run_hosted_editor_pura_live_tests.mjs.

**Remaining**
- I don’t see any blocker left in this hardening pass.
- The only next-step item I’d still consider is deeper Prisma/query tuning for overall latency, but the current production smoke gates are green.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node <<'NODE'
const BASE_URL = process.env.PURA_BASE_URL || 'http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const DEMO_EMAIL = process.env.PURA_DEMO_EMAIL || 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const DEMO_PASSWORD = process.env.PURA_DEMO_PASSWORD || 'pura-demo-local';

function extractCookie(setCookieHeader) {
  if (!setCookieHeader) return '';
  const first = String(setCookieHeader).split(',').find((part) => part.includes('=')) || '';
  return first.split(';')[0]?.trim() || '';
}
async function request(path, { method = 'GET', cookie = '', body } = {}) {
  const res = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}${path}`, {
    method,
    headers: {
      ...(cookie ? { cookie } : null),
      ...(body ? { 'content-type': 'application/json' } : null),
      ...(path.startsWith('/portal/api/login') ? { 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' } : null),
    },
    body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
  });
  const text = await res.text();
  let json = null;
  try { json = JSON.parse(text); } catch { json = { rawText: text }; }
  return { res, json };
}
(async () => {
  const login = await request('/portal/api/login', { method: 'POST', body: { email: DEMO_EMAIL, password: DEMO_PASSWORD } });
  const cookie = extractCookie(login.res.headers.get('set-cookie'));
  if (!login.res.ok || !login.json?.ok || !cookie) throw new Error(`login failed ${login.res.status}`);
  const thread = await request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads', { method: 'POST', cookie, body: { title: 'context-key smoke', chatMode: 'work', responseProfile: 'balanced' } });
  const threadId = thread.json?.thread?.id;
  if (!thread.res.ok || !thread.json?.ok || !threadId) throw new Error(`thread create failed ${thread.res.status}`);
  const send = await request(`/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/${encodeURIComponent(threadId)}/messages`, {
    method: 'POST',
    cookie,
    body: {
      text: 'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.',
      url: `${BASE_URL}/portal/app/services/reviews/page-editor`,
      canvasUrl: `${BASE_URL}/portal/app/services/reviews/page-editor`,
      contextKeys: ['reviews'],
      chatMode: 'work',
      responseProfile: 'balanced',
    },
  });
  const out = {
    status: send.res.status,
    ok: !!send.json?.ok,
    canvasUrl: send.json?.canvasUrl ?? null,
    runTraceSteps: Array.isArray(send.json?.runTrace?.steps) ? send.json.runTrace.steps.map((s) => s?.key ?? null) : [],
    assistantText: typeof send.json?.assistantMessage?.text === 'string' ? send.json.assistantMessage.text : null,
  };
  console.log(JSON.stringify(out, null, 2));
})();
NODE

DEMO_PASSWORD=process.env.PURA_DEMO_PASSWORD||'pura-demo-local';const extractCookie=h=>{if(!h)return '';const first=String(h).split(',').find(p=>p.includes('='))||'';return first.split(';')[0]?.trim()||'';};const request=async(path,{method='GET',cookie='',body}={})=>{const res=await fetch(BASE_URL+path,{method,headers:{...(cookie?{cookie}:{}),...(body?{'content-type':'application/json'}:{}),...(path.startsWith('/portal/api/login')?{'x-portal-variant':'portal'}:{})},body:body?JSON.stringify(body):undefined});const text=await res.text();let json;try{json=JSON.parse(text);}catch{json={rawText:text};}return {res,json};};(async()=>{const login=await request('/portal/api/login',{method:'POST',body:{email:DEMO_EMAIL,password:DEMO_PASSWORD}});const cookie=extractCookie(login.res.headers.get('set-cookie'));if(!login.res.ok||!login.json?.ok||!cookie)throw new Error('login failed');const thread=await request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads',{method:'POST',cookie,body:{title:'context-key smoke',chatMode:'work',responseProfile:'balanced'}});const threadId=thread.json?.thread?.id;if(!thread.res.ok||!thread.json?.ok||!threadId)throw new Error('thread create failed');const send=await request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/'+encodeURIComponent(threadId)+'/messages',{method:'POST',cookie,body:{text:'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.',url:BASE_URL+'/portal/app/services/reviews/page-editor',canvasUrl:BASE_URL+'/portal/app/services/reviews/page-editor',contextKeys:['reviews'],chatMode:'work',responseProfile:'balanced'}});const out={status:send.res.status,ok:!!send.json?.ok,canvasUrl:send.json?.canvasUrl??null,runTraceSteps:Array.isArray(send.json?.runTrace?.steps)?send.json.runTrace.steps.map(s=>s?.key??null):[],assistantText:typeof send.json?.assistantMessage?.text==='string'?send.json.assistantMessage.text:null};console.log(JSON.stringify(out,null,2));})();"

Send some prompts to Pura again, look at the responses, and ask yourself, "This shit, I would be happy with." Attach the context or use the selectors to make sure that it's actually picking out the text. If it's saying, "If I type something about funnel builder," make sure that it's understanding. Oh yeah, you type something, "Funnel builder", let's suggest this, and then they can click on it. It'll turn it fucking blue or something, blue and bold, so it looks like it's just that specific word or whatever. It'll say fucking funnel builder connected or whatever service connected, basically just to indicate that there's a service connected. It'll just highlight whatever word they used to indicate that there's a service connected. Basically, just to indicate that there's a service connected, it'll just highlight whatever word they used. That's what you need to do. If that's not already happening and you need to do all these tests and you need to make sure that everything is working very smoothly, just keep on doing that. Once you're a hundred percent confident you've done all these tests, you keep going back and forth with Pura trying a bunch of different prompts until there's literally nothing you can do and you're like, "Yeah, this thing is just great at what it does. Every response is awesome, nothing I can really change." Then we'll work on a commit, and I got some instructions for you there, but go ahead and do all that. Do as much testing as possible. I'm not talking about just stupid testing. I'm talking about real: sending prompts, actually looking at it, observing the states, take screenshots, look at the screenshots, see what's going on. Do everything you need to do.

**Done**
- Tightened active-surface intent anchoring in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts) so vague “make this more premium” prompts stay attached to the current hosted-page editor.
- Upgraded phrase-level service connectors in PortalAiChatClient.tsx so typed phrases like “funnel builder” become the visible connected token and still send the right `contextKeys`.
- Fixed hosted-page prompt handling in hostedPageGeneration.ts so explicit audience overrides like “for HVAC owners” are treated as authoritative instead of triggering redundant clarification.
- Cleaned hosted-page result summaries in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts so raw placeholder tokens no longer leak into assistant copy.

**Validated**
- `npm run pura:production-smoke` passed `9/9`.
- `npm run pura:editor-live-smoke` passed all 6 editor cases after the last patch.
- `npm run pura:hosted-live-smoke` passed all hosted-page cases; the remaining ambiguous no-surface case still intentionally behaves as a generic inbox lookup.
- The direct newsletter repro in repro_newsletter_edit_after_patch.json now updates the existing draft cleanly with no old funeral/HVAC reconfirmation question.
- Browser validation artifacts for the connector UX are in pura-service-connectors.

If you want, I can keep going on the last broader rough edge: improving totally vague plain-chat prompts when there is no active work surface.

The no-surface gap is closed without breaking same-thread hosted follow-ups. I’m marking the last steps done and then I’ll hand you the exact delta plus what remains, if anything.

Completed: *Reproduce vague plain-chat drift* (4/6)

**Done**
- Added a server-side ambiguity guard in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts) so fresh-thread prompts like “Clean this up and make it feel more premium” now ask for a target instead of drifting into inbox work.
- Persisted the last hosted-page target in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts and surfaced it to planning in puraPlanner.ts plus puraDirectIntentSignals.ts.
- Reused that hosted-page context so same-thread vague follow-ups now stay on the prior hosted page instead of misrouting into Funnel Builder.

**Validated**
- `node tmp/run_pura_hosted_template_live_tests.mjs` still passes the 5 hosted-page generation cases, and the ambiguous fresh-thread case now returns a clean clarification.
- A live same-thread regression check now does: reviews hosted page → “Clean this up...” → `hosted_pages.documents.generate_html` again, with no funnel/inbox drift.
- No new file errors in the touched files.

If you want, I can keep pushing on broader plain-chat quality next and run a few more real prompts through Pura.

Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation",
  "workingDirectory": "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "import json\nimport urllib.request\n\nBASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'\nEMAIL = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'\nPASSWORD = 'pura-demo-local'\n\n\ndef request(path, method='GET', cookie='', body=None):\n    data = json.dumps(body).encode('utf-8') if body is not None else None\n    req = urllib.request.Request(BASE_URL + path, data=data, method=method)\n    if cookie:\n        req.add_header('cookie', cookie)\n    if body is not None:\n        req.add_header('content-type', 'application/json')\n    if path.startswith('/portal/api/login'):\n        req.add_header('x-portal-variant', 'portal')\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as res:\n        text = res.read().decode('utf-8')\n        return res, json.loads(text)\n\nres, _ = request('/portal/api/login', method='POST', body={'email': EMAIL, 'password': PASSWORD})\ncookie = res.headers.get('set-cookie', '').split(';', 1)[0]\n\ncases = [\n    {\n        'id': 'plain-ambiguous',\n        'messages': [\n            {'text': 'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.'}\n        ],\n    },\n    {\n        'id': 'hosted-followup-same-thread',\n        'messages': [\n            {'text': 'Make my reviews page feel more concierge and premium. Keep it in draft and summarize the changes.'},\n            {'text': 'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.'},\n        ],\n    },\n    {\n        'id': 'contextkey-reviews-ambiguous',\n        'messages': [\n            {'text': 'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.', 'contextKeys': ['reviews']}\n        ],\n    },\n    {\n        'id': 'contextkey-funnel-copy',\n        'messages': [\n            {'text': 'Help me with funnel builder headline and landing page copy.', 'contextKeys': ['funnel-builder']}\n        ],\n    },\n    {\n        'id': 'contextkey-newsletter-tighten',\n        'messages': [\n            {'text': 'Tighten this and make it more premium.', 'contextKeys': ['newsletter']}\n        ],\n    },\n]\n\nresults = []\nfor case in cases:\n    _, thread_payload = request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'title': case['id'], 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\n    thread_id = thread_payload['thread']['id']\n    responses = []\n    for message in case['messages']:\n        payload = {\n            'text': message['text'],\n            'url': f'{BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat',\n            'chatMode': 'work',\n            'responseProfile': 'balanced',\n        }\n        if message.get('contextKeys'):\n            payload['contextKeys'] = message['contextKeys']\n        _, response = request(f'/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/{thread_id}/messages', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body=payload)\n        responses.append({\n            'text': message['text'],\n            'contextKeys': message.get('contextKeys'),\n            'trace': response.get('runTrace', {}).get('steps', []),\n            'assistantText': response.get('assistantMessage', {}).get('text'),\n            'canvasUrl': response.get('canvasUrl'),\n        })\n    results.append({'id': case['id'], 'responses': responses})\n\nprint(json.dumps(results, indent=2))"
}

  "codeSnippet": "import json\nimport urllib.request\n\nBASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'\nEMAIL = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'\nPASSWORD = 'pura-demo-local'\n\n\ndef request(path, method='GET', cookie='', body=None):\n    data = json.dumps(body).encode('utf-8') if body is not None else None\n    req = urllib.request.Request(BASE_URL + path, data=data, method=method)\n    if cookie:\n        req.add_header('cookie', cookie)\n    if body is not None:\n        req.add_header('content-type', 'application/json')\n    if path.startswith('/portal/api/login'):\n        req.add_header('x-portal-variant', 'portal')\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as res:\n        text = res.read().decode('utf-8')\n        return res, json.loads(text)\n\nres, _ = request('/portal/api/login', method='POST', body={'email': EMAIL, 'password': PASSWORD})\ncookie = res.headers.get('set-cookie', '').split(';', 1)[0]\n_, thread_payload = request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'title': 'hosted-followup', 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\nthread_id = thread_payload['thread']['id']\nrequest(f'/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/{thread_id}/messages', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'text': 'Make my reviews page feel more concierge and premium. Keep it in draft and summarize the changes.', 'url': f'{BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat', 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\n_, response = request(f'/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/{thread_id}/messages', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'text': 'Clean this up and make it feel more premium.', 'url': f'{BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat', 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\nprint(json.dumps({'trace': response.get('runTrace', {}).get('steps', []), 'assistantText': response.get('assistantMessage', {}).get('text')}, indent=2))"
}

Ran terminal command:  npm run pura:production-smoke

**Updated**
- Tightened hosted-service context handling in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts) so selected context like `reviews` or `newsletter` now treats vague polish prompts as hosted-page work instead of drifting into settings or inbox actions.
- Persisted the last hosted-page target in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts, exposed it in puraPlanner.ts, and typed it in puraDirectIntentSignals.ts so same-thread follow-ups stay anchored.

**Validated**
- Fresh thread, no surface: “Clean this up and make it feel more premium.” now cleanly asks for a target.
- Same thread after hosted-page work: the same vague follow-up now re-runs `hosted_pages.documents.generate_html` on the prior hosted page.
- Selected context only: `reviews` + vague prompt now refreshes the Reviews hosted page; `newsletter` + vague prompt now refreshes the Newsletter hosted page.
- Broader regression check passed: `npm run pura:production-smoke` → `9/9 passed`.

One remaining nuance is that `funnel-builder` context with no saved funnel/page in-thread still returns a prerequisite message, which is logically correct. If you want, I can keep going with browser-side prompt testing for that path next.

  "codeSnippet": "import json\nimport urllib.request\n\nBASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'\nEMAIL = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev'\nPASSWORD = 'pura-demo-local'\n\n\ndef request(path, method='GET', cookie='', body=None):\n    data = json.dumps(body).encode('utf-8') if body is not None else None\n    req = urllib.request.Request(BASE_URL + path, data=data, method=method)\n    if cookie:\n        req.add_header('cookie', cookie)\n    if body is not None:\n        req.add_header('content-type', 'application/json')\n    if path.startswith('/portal/api/login'):\n        req.add_header('x-portal-variant', 'portal')\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as res:\n        text = res.read().decode('utf-8')\n        return res, json.loads(text)\n\nres, _ = request('/portal/api/login', method='POST', body={'email': EMAIL, 'password': PASSWORD})\ncookie = res.headers.get('set-cookie', '').split(';', 1)[0]\nfor prompt in [\n    'Make my reviews page feel more concierge and premium. Keep it in draft and summarize the changes.',\n    'Make my newsletter editorial for HVAC owners and keep it in draft. Use publish-ready copy and tell me what changed.',\n    'Mak my booking page minimul but still proffesional. Do not publish it. Tell me exactly what changed.',\n]:\n    _, thread_payload = request('/api/portal/ai-chat/threads', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'title': 'copy-check', 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\n    thread_id = thread_payload['thread']['id']\n    _, response = request(f'/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/{thread_id}/messages', method='POST', cookie=cookie, body={'text': prompt, 'url': f'{BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat', 'chatMode': 'work', 'responseProfile': 'balanced'})\n    print('\\nPROMPT:', prompt)\n    print(response.get('assistantMessage', {}).get('text'))\n"
}

**Finished**
- Cleaned hosted-page reply wording in portalAgentActionExecutor.ts: no raw page keys like `reviews_concierge`, no em dashes, no bogus CTA extraction like `Facebook`, and low-signal fragments like `Newsletter`, `Heartfelt Reviews for`, or `Book with` are now filtered out.
- Added visible attached-context support in PortalAiChatClient.tsx: user messages now carry UI badges for attached service/page context instead of only sending hidden planner context.
- Kept the routing fixes in place so vague prompts still clarify cleanly in a fresh thread, stay anchored in same-thread hosted-page work, and use selected hosted service context correctly.

**Validated**
- Focused live checks now produce cleaner replies like “Reviews concierge page, draft, Custom HTML” with sane section summaries and no ugly internal labels.
- `node tmp/run_pura_hosted_template_live_tests.mjs` passes after the copy cleanup.
- `npm run pura:production-smoke` still passes `9/9`.
- Browser-side artifacts were captured in pura-attached-context and pura-service-connectors.

**What Changed In Practice**
- Ambiguous attached-context messages no longer read like contextless nonsense in the response path.
- Hosted-page summaries are more human and less internal.
- The chat client now has explicit UI support for showing attached context on user messages, alongside existing file/image attachments.

const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async()=>{
  const browser = await chromium.launch({headless:true});
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1512, height: 982 } });
  const res = await context.request.post('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/api/login', {
    headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' },
    data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'pura-demo-local' },
    failOnStatusCode: false,
    timeout: 120000,
  });
  if (!res.ok()) throw new Error(`login failed ${res.status()} ${await res.text()}`);
  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/ai-chat', { waitUntil:'domcontentloaded', timeout:120000 });
  const textarea = page.getByPlaceholder(/tell pura what you want handled|message/i).first();
  await textarea.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 120000 });
  const box = await textarea.boundingBox();
  await page.screenshot({ path:'tmp/review-shots/pura-service-connectors/current-composer-full.png', fullPage:true });
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ box, url: page.url() }, null, 2));
  await browser.close();
})();

`Smoke: pura production after composer width fix` task finished

**What Happened**
- Yeah — I see exactly what I broke. In PortalAiChatClient.tsx, I wrapped the textarea so I could measure the trigger-word position for the popover.
- Before that, the textarea itself was the flex item, so `flex-1` made it stretch normally. After the wrapper, the textarea was no longer the flex item, so it fell back to the browser’s default intrinsic textarea width, which is why it looked tiny.

**Fix**
- I restored the original sizing behavior by making the wrapper the flex item and the textarea explicitly fill it: `min-w-0 flex-1` on the wrapper, `w-full min-w-0` on the textarea.
- The liquid-glass popover stays in place; the textbox sizing regression is what I removed.

**Validation**
- PortalAiChatClient.tsx has no diagnostics.
- `npm run pura:production-smoke` passes `9/9`.

**Screenshots**
- I also tightened the capture script in capture_pura_service_connectors.mjs to grab better composer and connected-state shots.
- The recapture itself is currently blocked by the local demo auth request hanging on `/portal/api/login`, so I don’t want to fake “good” screenshots for you. If you want, I can keep pushing on that capture path next and regenerate the new-chat + connected-state images as soon as auth responds.

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const browser = await chromium.launch({headless:true});
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1512, height: 982 } });
const page = await context.newPage();
const res = await context.request.post(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`, { headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, data:{ email:'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password:'pura-demo-local' }, failOnStatusCode:false, timeout:120000 });
console.log('login', res.status());
await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/portal/app/ai-chat`, { waitUntil:'domcontentloaded', timeout:120000 });
const newChatButton = page.getByRole('button', { name:/new chat/i }).first();
if (await newChatButton.isVisible().catch(()=>false)) await newChatButton.click();
const composer = page.getByPlaceholder(/tell pura what you want handled|message/i).first();
await composer.waitFor({ state:'visible', timeout:120000 });
await composer.click();
await composer.pressSequentially('Can you help me improve booking confirmation and calendar messaging?');
await page.waitForTimeout(2500);
const buttons = await page.locator('button').evaluateAll((nodes)=>nodes.map((node)=>({text:(node.textContent||'').trim(), aria:node.getAttribute('aria-label'), title:node.getAttribute('title')})).filter((entry)=>/connect|booking|calendar|appointment/i.test(`${entry.text} ${entry.aria||''} ${entry.title||''}`)));
const popover = await page.locator('[data-composer-context-popover]').count();
console.log(JSON.stringify({ popover, buttons }, null, 2));
await browser.close();
EOF

- In the actual thread, I wanted you to give me a screenshot before you sent it in an actual thread, so type another message and let me see how it shows up there. You get what I'm saying.

const prompt = 'Can you help me with the funnel builder headline and the landing page copy?'.trim().toLowerCase();

const pageContext = /\b(funnel builder|landing page|sales page|page copy|hero section)\b/.test(prompt);

console.log({prompt, asksForHelp, copyTarget, pageContext, explicitBuildAction, result: asksForHelp && copyTarget && pageContext && !explicitBuildAction});
EOF

import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext();
const loginRes = await context.request.post(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`, { headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, data:{ email:'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password:'pura-demo-local' }, failOnStatusCode:false, timeout:120000 });
const createRes = await context.request.post(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/ai-chat/threads`, { headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'}, data:{ chatMode:'plan', responseProfile:'balanced' }, failOnStatusCode:false, timeout:120000 });
const createJson = await createRes.json().catch(()=>null);
console.log(JSON.stringify({ login: loginRes.status(), createStatus:createRes.status(), thread:createJson?.thread }, null, 2));
await browser.close();
EOF

I’m at a narrow blocker now: the UI fix and the discuss-mode fix are both done and verified, but the local Playwright environment is intermittently refusing to surface/open the existing thread row for the final unsent-draft screenshot. I’m updating the task state accordingly and I’ll hand you the concrete fixes plus the exact blocker.

Starting: *Capture actual thread draft state* (2/3)

**Fixed**
- Updated PortalAiChatClient.tsx so the connect bubble is now tinted a very light blue, including the triangle, instead of the white glass treatment.
- Reworked the connected-text treatment so it no longer does the full duplicated-line overlay; it now keeps the line aligned and only renders the matched phrase with the blue/bold highlight treatment.
- Fixed the bad discuss-mode behavior in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts) and puraDirectIntentSignals.ts: this was not the system prompt causing the stupid “create landing page” answer, it was the direct-intent/preflight routing path firing before a proper discuss response. I verified the new behavior in discuss-funnel-copy-response.json.

**What changed on the discuss bug**
- The route was treating that prompt like mutating funnel work too early, then falling into either a prerequisite check or a generic discuss/work deflection.
- I added two guardrails:
- advisory copy/headline asks no longer set the funnel-page direct-intent flags so aggressively
- discuss mode now short-circuits those prompts into actual advisory copy guidance instead of work-mode gating

**Screenshots**
- 01-new-chat-funnel-builder-full.png
- 02-new-chat-booking-full.png
- 03-new-chat-multi-full.png
- 04-connected-draft-full.png
- 05-actual-thread-full.png

**One blocker**
- The only flaky piece left is overwriting the thread screenshot with the exact “existing thread + unsent new draft typed in composer” state every time via Playwright; the local thread-row discovery/navigation is timing out even though the thread exists through the API.
- The code for that capture is updated in capture_pura_remaining_states.mjs and capture_actual_thread_draft_only.mjs, but the local browser run is the current blocker.

If you want, I can keep going specifically on that last screenshot-capture flake until the unsent-draft thread shot is overwritten cleanly too.

GitHub Copilot: I’m just confirming the exact guard in the composer logic so I can answer precisely. Then I’ll tell you the behavior in plain English.

Read [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx#660-660), lines 660 to 710

Read [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx#4528-4528), lines 4528 to 4568

I found the guard. I’m grabbing the call site too so I can confirm it’s using the currently connected services, not just raw text matching.

Read [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/src/app/portal/app/ai-chat/PortalAiChatClient.tsx#4568-4568), lines 4568 to 4605

Yes — that is how it works now.

- Once Funnel Builder is already connected for the current draft, it is added to the selected service slugs passed into the suggestion engine in PortalAiChatClient.tsx.
- The suggestion engine explicitly skips any already-selected service: if the slug is in the selected set, it returns a negative score and that service is filtered out, so it will not be suggested again in that draft in PortalAiChatClient.tsx.
- So if you connect Funnel Builder, then keep typing “funnel” again, it should not pop the connector again unless you disconnect it or start from a draft/thread state where it is no longer selected.

So your expected behavior is correct.

**Mode Switch**
- Yes — if you stay in the same thread, switch from Discuss to Work, and then say something like “now get started on this plan,” that is the intended handoff and it should work well.
- The UI persists the thread’s mode when you flip it in PortalAiChatClient.tsx, and the server stores that mode on the thread in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/actions/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/actions/route.ts#L147-L158).
- On the next message, the send route reads the stored thread mode and uses it unless you explicitly override it, in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts#L3355-L3368).
- It also builds the planner prompt with recent messages, thread summary, unresolved work, next-step context, and continuation hints in [src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/[threadId]/messages/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/ai-chat/threads/%5BthreadId%5D/messages/route.ts#L6774-L6788) and portalAiChatPlannerShared.ts.
- So: same thread + explicit “start/continue/keep going” instruction = strong continuity. The only caveat is that switching to Work does not auto-run by itself; you still need to send the follow-up message.

**Popover Triggers**
- Matching is case-insensitive and boundary-aware, so it looks for whole words/phrases rather than random substrings, in PortalAiChatClient.tsx.
- The popover can trigger from a service title, the normalized slug phrase, or the explicit keyword list in PortalAiChatClient.tsx and PortalAiChatClient.tsx.

- Funnel Builder: Funnel Builder, funnel builder, funnel, landing page, checkout page, upsell page, downsell page, thank you page, page builder
- Inbox / Outbox: Inbox / Outbox, inbox, sms, text thread, reply, conversation, email inbox, email thread
- Media Library: Media Library, media library, asset library, image library, video library, upload
- Tasks: Tasks, tasks, task, to do, todo, follow up task, reminder
- AI Receptionist: AI Receptionist, ai receptionist, missed call, voicemail, call handling, phone
- Newsletter: Newsletter, newsletter, email campaign, broadcast, email blast
- Booking Automation: Booking Automation, booking, calendar, appointment, availability, scheduler
- Lead Scraping: Lead Scraping, lead scraping
- Automation Builder: Automation Builder, automations, automation, workflow, sequence, automated follow up
- Automated Blogs: Automated Blogs, blogs, blog, article, seo blog, blog post
- Reviews: Reviews, reviews, review, testimonial, google review, rating
- Nurture Campaigns: Nurture Campaigns, nurture campaigns
- Reporting: Reporting, reporting

- Also, as you already spotted, once a service is already connected for that draft, it is filtered out and will not be suggested again in PortalAiChatClient.tsx.

If you want, I can also turn that trigger list into a tiny doc in docs/ so you have a permanent reference.

Done — I added the broader popover triggers, added the schedule-specific popover action, and made the disabled send button stay visually inert.

**What Changed**
- Updated the trigger logic in PortalAiChatClient.tsx so the composer can now show either:
  - normal service-connect suggestions, or
  - a schedule shortcut that says `Schedule this task`
- Clicking `Schedule this task` now opens the existing schedule modal and pre-fills it with the current prompt text, so it behaves like the plus-menu scheduling flow without extra steps.
- The send button now only does the hover/icon animation when it can actually send; if the composer is empty, it stays disabled-looking and non-animated.

**New Trigger Words Added**
- Funnel Builder: `website`, `website page`, `website settings`, `funnel settings`, `page settings`, `site`
- Inbox: `messages`, `messaging`, `inbox settings`
- Newsletter: `email marketing`, `campaign`, `newsletter settings`, `email settings`, `audience`
- Booking: `bookings`, `booking settings`, `calendar settings`, `appointment settings`
- Media Library: `uploads`, `assets`, `files`, `photos`, `videos`
- Tasks: `checklist`, `action item`, `follow-up reminder`
- Automations: `trigger`, `logic`, `automation settings`, `workflow settings`
- Blogs: `content`, `seo content`, `post`
- Reviews: `reputation`, `trust`, `social proof`, `credibility`, `customer feedback`, `review settings`
- AI Receptionist: `incoming call`, `calls`, `receptionist`, `answer calls`, `phone settings`
- AI Outbound: `call`, `calls`, `outbound`, `outbound call`, `call campaign`, `dial`, `dialing`, `phone outreach`, `cold call`, `follow up call`
- Lead Scraping: `lead`, `leads`, `prospect`, `prospects`, `lead list`, `prospect list`, `target leads`, `scrape leads`
- Nurture Campaigns: `nurture campaign`, `nurture`, `lead nurture`, `drip campaign`, `drip`, `re-engagement`, `long-term follow up`, `follow-up campaign`
- Reporting: `report`, `reports`, `analytics`, `metrics`, `dashboard`, `performance`, `tracking`, `reporting settings`

**Schedule Triggers**
- Added scheduling phrases like `schedule`, `scheduled`, `schedule task`, `remind me`, `set a reminder`, `every day`, `every week`, `every month`, `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`, `recurring`, `repeat this`, `run this later`, `automatically every`

**Validation**
- Diagnostics are clean for PortalAiChatClient.tsx.
