# dev-retro vs 6d9398d3452a2925d8539e5df389d2be59729d44

## Comparison scope

- Baseline commit: `6d9398d3452a2925d8539e5df389d2be59729d44`
- Baseline timestamp: `April 12, 2026 at 2:28:48 PM (UTC-07:00)`
- Comparison branch: `dev-retro`
- Current branch head: `37ea3b4512ef934b71e8d6709b84c637b43e5583`
- Commit(s) since baseline:
  - `37ea3b45` `Polish portal behavior and dashboard defaults`

## Diff summary

- Files changed: `33`
- Insertions: `3535`
- Deletions: `865`
- New files added:
  - `src/lib/portalAiOutboundIntelligence.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalBlogsPreview.client.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalUiPreview.client.ts`

## Executive summary

This branch is a broad portal polish pass with four main tracks:

1. AI outbound calls were made more structured, less prompt-fragile, and more context-aware.
2. Dashboard defaults, layout behavior, shell interaction, and scrollbar behavior were reworked to reduce empty space, hover jitter, and sidebar instability.
3. Blog, reviews, and preview-side portal flows were expanded to better support local preview, editing, and embedded/sidebar-driven operation.
4. A set of runtime and interaction bugs were addressed during the session, including a billing runtime reference issue, a dashboard hooks-order issue, and a sidebar snap-back bug in blog post editing.

## Major change areas

### 1. AI outbound calls and messaging intelligence

Core intent of this work:

- reduce reliance on the user being unusually good at prompting
- make outbound behavior safer and more structured in pacing, replies, and next-step handling
- unify preview behavior and production behavior
- derive more intelligence from existing business profile and campaign context

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalAiOutboundIntelligence.ts` as a shared intelligence layer.
- Strengthened outbound prompt generation in:
  - `src/lib/elevenLabsConvai.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/generate-agent-config/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/preview-message-reply/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/cron/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/sync-agent/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/sync-chat-agent/route.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalAgentActionExecutor.ts`
- Added context-strength analysis and narrower prompt-blocking logic so the system only requires extra user input when both offer clarity and next-step clarity are missing.
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/services/ai-outbound-calls/PortalAiOutboundCallsClient.tsx` to surface context insight back to the user.

Net effect:

- preview and production behavior are closer to each other
- outbound generation is more opinionated and safer
- the system uses existing profile/context more aggressively before asking for more user input

### 2. Dashboard defaults, layout logic, and shell behavior

Core intent of this work:

- stop the dashboard from feeling sparse, left-heavy, and unfinished by default
- make the shell/sidebar behavior calmer and closer to Apple-style interaction patterns
- improve what happens when the sidebar collapses so content actually benefits from the released space

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts` as a shared dashboard layout engine.
- Reworked default dashboard configuration in `src/lib/portalDashboard.ts`.
- Rebuilt major portions of `src/app/portal/PortalDashboardClient.tsx`:
  - stronger default widget mix
  - richer widgets including `puraAttention` and `activityPulse`
  - sparse-dashboard fallback suggestions when a saved dashboard has too few widgets
  - calmer edit controls and card interactions
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/page.tsx` to widen the dashboard stage.
- Updated `src/app/globals.css`, `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`, and `src/app/portal/PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx` to reduce hover jitter, sidebar chip movement, and Windows-like scrollbar intrusion.
- Updated `src/app/portal/PortalFloatingTools.tsx` and `src/app/portal/PortalThemeClient.tsx` as part of broader shell and portal polish.

Net effect:

- default dashboard behavior is less empty and less visually broken
- sidebar collapse has more meaningful payoff because the dashboard stage is wider
- portal scroll surfaces feel more consistent

### 3. Blogs, preview helpers, and editing flows

Core intent of this work:

- improve blog editing and preview behavior
- improve route-specific portal behavior during authoring/editing
- support better local preview handling

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalBlogsPreview.client.ts`
- Added `src/lib/portalUiPreview.client.ts`
- Updated blog-related routes and clients:
  - `src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/PortalBlogsClient.tsx`
  - `src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx`
  - `src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/blogs/[slug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/domain-router/[domain]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/lib/blog.ts`
- Fixed the blog-post editor sidebar glitch by making force-collapsed sidebar state behave as a real lock in `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`.

Net effect:

- blog editing behavior is more stable
- preview support is more explicit in shared helpers
- the portal shell no longer lies to the user by allowing an expand action that will instantly snap back

### 4. Billing, reviews, profile, and supporting portal fixes

Core intent of this work:

- remove runtime/interaction failures uncovered during the session
- strengthen adjacent portal surfaces touched by the dashboard and outbound work

Key implementation changes:

- Fixed a runtime reference-order issue in `src/app/portal/billing/PortalBillingClient.tsx` by hoisting `setupHrefForService`.
- Updated `src/app/portal/profile/BusinessProfileForm.tsx`.
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/services/reviews/setup/PortalReviewsClient.tsx`.
- Updated `src/app/api/portal/engagement/active-time/route.ts`, `src/lib/portalActiveTime.client.ts`, and `src/app/api/portal/media/stats/route.ts`.
- Updated `src/app/(auth)/login/PortalLoginClient.tsx`.

Net effect:

- the portal is less brittle in a few runtime-sensitive areas that surfaced during iterative testing

## Runtime and regression fixes applied during the session

These were not just broad feature changes; they also included targeted corrections found while iterating:

- Fixed React hook-order violation in `src/app/portal/PortalDashboardClient.tsx`.
- Fixed blog editor sidebar snap-back caused by force-collapsed override behavior in `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`.
- Fixed billing runtime reference issue at `setupHrefForService` in `src/app/portal/billing/PortalBillingClient.tsx`.

Important focus areas in `dev-retro`:
- outbound AI intelligence and prompt/runtime alignment
- dashboard layout/default widget behavior
- portal shell/sidebar/scroll behavior
- blogs preview/editor behavior
- runtime bug fixes in billing and dashboard

await browser.close();

Ran terminal command:  git diff 6d9398d3452a2925d8539e5df389d2be59729d44..origin/dev-retro -- src/lib/portalDashboard.ts src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts | sed -n '1,260p'

Yep — for the next pass I would keep it to the dashboard bundle only, and if we do screenshots after that I’ll keep them full-page only, no close-ups.

**Tier Two**
- PortalDashboardClient.tsx: this is the big user-facing change. It makes the dashboard feel more “alive” and useful instead of mostly static cards.
- portalDashboard.ts: this changes the default dashboard setup and how widgets get added/reset server-side.
- src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts: this is a new shared layout helper so the client and server stop hardcoding dashboard widget sizing/placement in multiple places.

**What Would Change**
- Two new dashboard cards get introduced: a “Pura attention” card and an “Activity pulse” card.
- “Pura attention” is basically a quick “what needs your focus right now” panel with links into the right areas.
- “Activity pulse” adds a compact recent-activity chart so the dashboard shows motion and trend, not just static counts.
- The default dashboard layout gets rebalanced around those higher-signal widgets, so the top of the page carries more useful context.
- Sparse dashboards would get “add widget” suggestions, so the page helps fill itself out instead of looking half-empty.
- Widget layout logic gets centralized, which is mostly an internal cleanup, but it makes add/remove/reset behavior more consistent.
- Dashboard loading becomes a bit smarter: the absolutely required data loads first, while slower reporting/media data becomes more optional, so the whole dashboard is less likely to fall over just because one slower endpoint drags.

**Risk Level**
- This is still relatively safe compared to the branch’s bigger editor/Prisma work, but it is definitely a step up from tier one because it changes real dashboard UX and behavior.
- The good news is it does not look like a schema migration or deep data-model change; it’s mostly dashboard UI, layout logic, and load behavior.

**My Recommendation**
- Safeest tier-two import: bring over the shared layout logic, the improved dashboard loading behavior, and the new top-level cards.
- Optional part to evaluate before import: the preview/mock-mode scaffolding in the dashboard client. That looks dev-oriented, so I’d probably trim that unless it helps an existing preview workflow.

If you want, I can do the next step now: isolate the exact “safe tier-two subset” from those three files before we implement it.

- The next clean chunk to bring in from `dev-retro` is the dashboard layout/data foundation, not more screenshot assets.
- That chunk is centered on portalDashboardLayout.ts and portalDashboard.ts.

- This is the right next handoff because it’s the smallest functional unit that makes the tier-two dashboard behavior portable without dragging in unrelated portal/editor work.

**What It Includes**

- portalDashboardLayout.ts
  - Defines the shared widget IDs
  - Encodes the layout presets per widget
  - Builds default grid placement in a reusable way
- portalDashboard.ts
  - Switches the dashboard defaults over to that shared layout builder
  - Adds the new default widget set/order

- Do not bring the screenshot output folder
- Do not bring the hosted page editor / portal glass / Pura planner work from that branch yet
- Do not bring unrelated portal shell changes unless we intentionally make a separate chunk

- First: portalDashboardLayout.ts
- Then: portalDashboard.ts
- After that, if you want the full visible dashboard feature set as a follow-up chunk, we’d bring the dashboard client/UI wiring in PortalDashboardClient.tsx

- shared modal/dropdown glass styling
- billing/contact/media polish
- sidebar icon hover/chip polish
- small service-surface refinements

const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const email='demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password='pura-demo-local';
function parseCookie(setCookie){const m=String(setCookie||'').match(/pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)/);return m?.[1]||''}
const loginRes=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`,{method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'},body:JSON.stringify({email,password})});
const cookie=parseCookie(loginRes.headers.get('set-cookie'));
const headers={cookie:`pa.portal.session=${cookie}`};

const postsJson=await postsRes.json();

const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async () => {
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const page = await browser.newPage({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1600 } });
  page.on('console', msg => console.log('console:', msg.type(), msg.text()));
  page.on('pageerror', err => console.log('pageerror:', err.message));
  page.on('response', async res => {
    const url = res.url();
    if (url.includes('/api/portal/newsletter/')) console.log('api:', res.status(), url);
  });
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/services/newsletter/external', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 120000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(15000);
  const text = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim());
  console.log('text:', text.slice(0, 1200));
  await browser.close();
})();
EOF

I found the missing piece: my quick browser probe wasn’t authenticated, so it was a false signal. I’m re-running the newsletter page with the same session cookie the capture script uses and checking the actual API responses.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node - <<'EOF'
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
function parseCookie(setCookie) {
  const match = String(setCookie || '').match(/pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)/);
  return match?.[1] || '';
}
(async () => {
  const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000';
  const loginRes = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'pura-demo-local' }),
  });
  const cookie = parseCookie(loginRes.headers.get('set-cookie'));
  console.log('login', loginRes.status, Boolean(cookie));
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1600 } });
  await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookie, domain: '127.0.0.1', path: '/', httpOnly: true, secure: false, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
  const page = await context.newPage();
  page.on('console', msg => console.log('console:', msg.type(), msg.text()));
  page.on('pageerror', err => console.log('pageerror:', err.message));
  page.on('response', res => {
    const url = res.url();
    if (url.includes('/api/portal/newsletter/')) console.log('api:', res.status(), url);
  });
  await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/portal/app/services/newsletter/external`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 120000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(20000);
  const text = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim());
  console.log('text:', text.slice(0, 1600));
  await browser.close();
})();
EOF

What I meant by “shared hosted-page / page-editor infrastructure”:
- It’s the reusable system behind the service page editors, not just one page.
- Concretely, that next `dev-retro` chunk is stuff like:
  - the shared hosted-page libs: hostedPageDocuments.ts, hostedPageGeneration.ts, hostedPageRuntime.tsx, hostedPageKeys.ts
  - the shared editor component: HostedServicePageEditorClient.tsx
  - the hosted-page API routes under documents
  - the service editor entry routes like the `page-editor` routes for blogs / newsletters / reviews / booking
- In plain English: it’s the common machinery that lets those services use one consistent hosted-page editor, preview, publish, export, and document model instead of each service reinventing it.

If you want, I’ll move straight into that hosted-page/editor import next.

GitHub Copilot: Yeah — mostly, yes, that is the stuff we’ve been working on. I was not talking about building some whole different editor from scratch again.

**What It Is**
- “Hosted page” means the public-facing pages your portal services publish to the web.
- Not the funnel builder editor.
- Think pages like:
  - blogs pages
  - newsletter pages
  - reviews page
  - booking/service pages
- Those are the pages that end up on routes like your public site / domain, not the internal portal UI itself.

**What It Is Not**
- Not the Pura chat window
- Not the dashboard
- Not the funnel builder page editor specifically
- Not a new concept separate from what we already built

**What I Meant**
- We already do have a lot of this built on the current branch.
- The remaining `dev-retro` chunk is more like: “make all those public service pages run through the same editing/publishing machinery consistently.”
- So the import would not be “build hosted pages.”
- It would be “bring over the remaining unification/refactor pieces from `dev-retro` that make blogs/newsletters/reviews/booking share the same editor backend and publish flow.”

**What That Change Would Look Like**
- More services using the same kind of page editor experience instead of each one having slightly separate behavior
- The same style of preview / publish / export / save draft behavior across those services
- The same underlying document model for those public pages
- Less duplicated one-off logic per service

**In Plain English**
- Funnel builder = build landing pages / funnels
- Hosted page editor = edit the regular public site pages for services like newsletters, reviews, blogs, booking
- The next `dev-retro` chunk would likely be polishing and unifying that system, not replacing the work we just did

So your instinct is right: this is basically the same area we just spent time on. I was pointing at the remaining `dev-retro` pieces around that area, not saying “we need to invent hosted-page editing now.”

User: Okay, so you need to pull what's on dev-retro based on some improvements that are there that we don't have here's an example of a document that he gave me of some changes that he made that we need to just see if we already pulled in or not:
Diff summary

- Files changed: `33`
- Insertions: `3535`
- Deletions: `865`
- New files added:
  - `src/lib/portalAiOutboundIntelligence.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalBlogsPreview.client.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalUiPreview.client.ts`

## Executive summary

This branch is a broad portal polish pass with four main tracks:

1. AI outbound calls were made more structured, less prompt-fragile, and more context-aware.
2. Dashboard defaults, layout behavior, shell interaction, and scrollbar behavior were reworked to reduce empty space, hover jitter, and sidebar instability.
3. Blog, reviews, and preview-side portal flows were expanded to better support local preview, editing, and embedded/sidebar-driven operation.
4. A set of runtime and interaction bugs were addressed during the session, including a billing runtime reference issue, a dashboard hooks-order issue, and a sidebar snap-back bug in blog post editing.

## Major change areas

### 1. AI outbound calls and messaging intelligence

Core intent of this work:

- reduce reliance on the user being unusually good at prompting
- make outbound behavior safer and more structured in pacing, replies, and next-step handling
- unify preview behavior and production behavior
- derive more intelligence from existing business profile and campaign context

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalAiOutboundIntelligence.ts` as a shared intelligence layer.
- Strengthened outbound prompt generation in:
  - `src/lib/elevenLabsConvai.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/generate-agent-config/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/preview-message-reply/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/cron/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/sync-agent/route.ts`
  - `src/app/api/portal/ai-outbound-calls/campaigns/[campaignId]/sync-chat-agent/route.ts`
  - `src/lib/portalAgentActionExecutor.ts`
- Added context-strength analysis and narrower prompt-blocking logic so the system only requires extra user input when both offer clarity and next-step clarity are missing.
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/services/ai-outbound-calls/PortalAiOutboundCallsClient.tsx` to surface context insight back to the user.

Net effect:

- preview and production behavior are closer to each other
- outbound generation is more opinionated and safer
- the system uses existing profile/context more aggressively before asking for more user input

### 2. Dashboard defaults, layout logic, and shell behavior

Core intent of this work:

- stop the dashboard from feeling sparse, left-heavy, and unfinished by default
- make the shell/sidebar behavior calmer and closer to Apple-style interaction patterns
- improve what happens when the sidebar collapses so content actually benefits from the released space

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalDashboardLayout.ts` as a shared dashboard layout engine.
- Reworked default dashboard configuration in `src/lib/portalDashboard.ts`.
- Rebuilt major portions of `src/app/portal/PortalDashboardClient.tsx`:
  - stronger default widget mix
  - richer widgets including `puraAttention` and `activityPulse`
  - sparse-dashboard fallback suggestions when a saved dashboard has too few widgets
  - calmer edit controls and card interactions
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/page.tsx` to widen the dashboard stage.
- Updated `src/app/globals.css`, `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`, and `src/app/portal/PortalServiceSidebarIcons.tsx` to reduce hover jitter, sidebar chip movement, and Windows-like scrollbar intrusion.
- Updated `src/app/portal/PortalFloatingTools.tsx` and `src/app/portal/PortalThemeClient.tsx` as part of broader shell and portal polish.

Net effect:

- default dashboard behavior is less empty and less visually broken
- sidebar collapse has more meaningful payoff because the dashboard stage is wider
- portal scroll surfaces feel more consistent

### 3. Blogs, preview helpers, and editing flows

Core intent of this work:

- improve blog editing and preview behavior
- improve route-specific portal behavior during authoring/editing
- support better local preview handling

Key implementation changes:

- Added `src/lib/portalBlogsPreview.client.ts`
- Added `src/lib/portalUiPreview.client.ts`
- Updated blog-related routes and clients:
  - `src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/PortalBlogsClient.tsx`
  - `src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx`
  - `src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/blogs/[slug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/domain-router/[domain]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx`
  - `src/lib/blog.ts`
- Fixed the blog-post editor sidebar glitch by making force-collapsed sidebar state behave as a real lock in `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`.

Net effect:

- blog editing behavior is more stable
- preview support is more explicit in shared helpers
- the portal shell no longer lies to the user by allowing an expand action that will instantly snap back

### 4. Billing, reviews, profile, and supporting portal fixes

Core intent of this work:

- remove runtime/interaction failures uncovered during the session
- strengthen adjacent portal surfaces touched by the dashboard and outbound work

Key implementation changes:

- Fixed a runtime reference-order issue in `src/app/portal/billing/PortalBillingClient.tsx` by hoisting `setupHrefForService`.
- Updated `src/app/portal/profile/BusinessProfileForm.tsx`.
- Updated `src/app/portal/app/services/reviews/setup/PortalReviewsClient.tsx`.
- Updated `src/app/api/portal/engagement/active-time/route.ts`, `src/lib/portalActiveTime.client.ts`, and `src/app/api/portal/media/stats/route.ts`.
- Updated `src/app/(auth)/login/PortalLoginClient.tsx`.

Net effect:

- the portal is less brittle in a few runtime-sensitive areas that surfaced during iterative testing

## Runtime and regression fixes applied during the session

These were not just broad feature changes; they also included targeted corrections found while iterating:

- Fixed React hook-order violation in `src/app/portal/PortalDashboardClient.tsx`.
- Fixed blog editor sidebar snap-back caused by force-collapsed override behavior in `src/app/portal/PortalShell.tsx`.
- Fixed billing runtime reference issue at `setupHrefForService` in `src/app/portal/billing/PortalBillingClient.tsx`.

for f in $files; do
  current=no
  remote=no
  [[ -e "$f" ]] && current=yes
  git cat-file -e "origin/dev-retro:$f" 2>/dev/null && remote=yes

  if [[ "$current" == yes && "$remote" == yes ]]; then
    if git diff --quiet origin/dev-retro -- "$f"; then
      status=same_as_dev_retro
    else
      status=differs_from_dev_retro
    fi
  elif [[ "$current" == yes && "$remote" == no ]]; then
    status=current_only
  elif [[ "$current" == no && "$remote" == yes ]]; then
    status=dev_retro_only
  else
    status=missing_both
  fi

  printf '%s|%s|%s|%s\n' "$status" "$current" "$remote" "$f"
done | sort
EOF
zsh /tmp/dev_retro_audit.sh

const baseUrl = 'http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const email = 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password = 'DemoPortal123!';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1400 } });
const page = await context.newPage();
try {
  const res = await context.request.post(`${baseUrl}/portal/api/login`, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, data: { email, password }, failOnStatusCode: false, timeout: 90000 });
  console.log('login', res.status());
  await page.goto(`${baseUrl}/portal/app/services/ai-outbound-calls/calls`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 90000 });

  console.log(text.slice(0, 4000));
  await page.screenshot({ path: 'tmp/review-shots/ai-outbound-dev-retro/debug-calls.png', fullPage: false });
} finally {
  await context.close();
  await browser.close();
}

const path = require('path');
const Module = require('module');
const origResolveFilename = Module._resolveFilename;
Module._resolveFilename = function patchedResolveFilename(request, parent, isMain, options) {
  if (typeof request === 'string' && request.startsWith('@/')) {
    request = path.join(process.cwd(), 'src', request.slice(2));
  }
  return origResolveFilename.call(this, request, parent, isMain, options);
};

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node --input-type=module - <<'EOF'
const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const email='demo-full@purelyautomation.dev';
const password='pura-demo-local';
const loginRes=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`,{method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json','x-portal-variant':'portal'},body:JSON.stringify({email,password})});
const loginJson=await loginRes.json().catch(()=>null);
const cookie=(loginRes.headers.get('set-cookie')||'').match(/pa\.portal\.session=([^;]+)/)?.[1]||'';
if(!cookie) throw new Error(`login failed ${loginRes.status} ${JSON.stringify(loginJson)}`);
for (const channel of ['email','sms']) {
  const res=await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/inbox/threads?channel=${channel}`,{headers:{cookie:`pa.portal.session=${cookie}`}});
  const json=await res.json().catch(()=>null);
  console.log(JSON.stringify({channel,status:res.status,ok:json?.ok,count:Array.isArray(json?.threads)?json.threads.length:null,sample:Array.isArray(json?.threads)?json.threads.slice(0,3).map(t=>({id:t.id,channel:t.channel,peerAddress:t.peerAddress,lastMessageDirection:t.lastMessageDirection})):null},null,2));
}
EOF

Made changes.

User: This is good, but on the top right, where it says “New text”, make the “Edit contact” button use the same pencil icon we use for edit. Get rid of the “New text” button, because we have that in the sidebar.

The rest of it is fine, but just fix that and then give me a screenshot of a thread. I think the rest of it is fine, as long as the search bar that pops up under that search bar has the liquid glass styling. That dropdown should have that styling whenever we’re searching.

For the filter thing, when I click the filter button, make sure that the popup has that liquid glass styling as well. Whatever I click in the filter (like “Last 7 days”, “Last 90 days”, or “Incoming only”) should be that light blue, and the toggle for “Incoming only” should be blue instead of that dark gray. It should be the same light blue we use for the “Done” button in our dashboard, and make sure it doesn’t have an outline on it.

Also show me the loaded threads and email. Before you click on one, show me what it looks like when the whole threads area is loaded.

That’s about it. From there, also make sure that in Pura and anywhere else, if I keep scrolling in the threads, I’m able to see all of them, all of my threads from all time. Right now, it’s going back, and I don’t know, there’s like a limit on how many threads will come. As I keep trying to scroll more, more should come up underneath so I can just keep scrolling all the way until my farthest back thread in time. That applies to email threads and SMS threads. If they have a whole lot of them, they should still be able to scroll, and it shouldn’t be a weird thing. Also, in SMS, before we select a thread, there shouldn't be a new text button in the top right just because we're already on a new text. If there isn't a thread selected, we don't need a new text button like that; that's just insane. And whenever you get me these screenshots of all this stuff, I need a screenshot of the chat and report widget opened. I need it opened to report, and I need it opened to chat as well. In some screenshots, don't crop any of them. I want the whole page in view.

Ran terminal command:  cd "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation" && node <<'EOF'
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
(async () => {
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1180, height: 1000 } });
  const res = await context.request.post('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/api/login', {
    timeout: 300000,
    headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' },
    data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'pura-demo-local' },
  });
  console.log('login', res.status());
  await context.route('**/api/portal/profile', async (route) => {
    const response = await route.fetch();
    const body = await response.json().catch(() => null);
    if (body && typeof body === 'object') {
      await route.fulfill({ response, json: { ...body, user: { ...(body.user || {}), hideFloatingTools: false } } });
      return;
    }
    await route.continue();
  });
  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.addInitScript(() => {
    localStorage.setItem('pa_portal_floating_tools_minimized', '1');
    localStorage.setItem('pa_portal_floating_tools_dock', 'pill');
    document.documentElement.removeAttribute('data-pa-hide-floating-tools');
    document.documentElement.removeAttribute('data-pa-hide-floating-tools-pref');
  });
  await page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:3000/portal/app/settings/appearance', { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
  const buttons = await page.evaluate(() => Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).map((button) => ({
    text: (button.textContent || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(),
    aria: button.getAttribute('aria-label') || '',
    visible: (() => {
      const style = getComputedStyle(button);
      const rect = button.getBoundingClientRect();
      return style.display !== 'none' && style.visibility !== 'hidden' && rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0;
    })(),
  })).filter((entry) => entry.visible && (entry.text || entry.aria)));
  console.log(JSON.stringify(buttons.slice(-20), null, 2));
  await browser.close();
})();
EOF

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node - <<'NODE'
const { chromium } = await import('@playwright/test');
const BASE_URL='http://127.0.0.1:3000';
const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
const context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 1200 } });
const res = await context.request.post(`${BASE_URL}/portal/api/login`, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json', 'x-portal-variant': 'portal' }, data: { email: 'demo-full@purelyautomation.dev', password: 'pura-demo-local' } });
const json = await res.json().catch(() => null);
if (!res.ok || !json?.ok) throw new Error(`login failed ${res.status}`);
const state = await context.storageState();
const cookie = state.cookies.find((entry) => entry.name === 'pa.portal.session');
await context.addCookies([{ name: 'pa.portal.session', value: cookie.value, url: BASE_URL, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Lax' }]);
const funnelsRes = await context.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/funnel-builder/funnels`, { headers: { cookie: `pa.portal.session=${cookie.value}` } });
const funnelsJson = await funnelsRes.json();
const funnel = (funnelsJson.funnels || []).find((item) => !/webinar audit sprint/i.test(String(item?.name || ''))) || funnelsJson.funnels?.[0];
const pagesRes = await context.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/api/portal/funnel-builder/funnels/${encodeURIComponent(funnel.id)}/pages`, { headers: { cookie: `pa.portal.session=${cookie.value}` } });
const pagesJson = await pagesRes.json();
const pageInfo = (pagesJson.pages || []).find((item) => String(item?.slug || '').toLowerCase() === 'home') || pagesJson.pages?.[0];
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/portal/app/services/funnel-builder/funnels/${encodeURIComponent(funnel.id)}/edit?pageId=${encodeURIComponent(pageInfo.id)}`, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', timeout: 60000 });
await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
const buttons = await page.evaluate(() => Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).map((button) => ({ text: (button.textContent || '').replace(/\s+/g,' ').trim(), aria: button.getAttribute('aria-label') || '', title: button.getAttribute('title') || '' })).filter((item) => item.text || item.aria || item.title).slice(0, 200));
console.log(JSON.stringify(buttons, null, 2));
await browser.close();
NODE
